abstract: 본 발명은, 짠맛은 줄이고 단맛을 증가시키며 점도를 진하게 제조할 수 있으면서 원하는 점도를 달성하기 위한 가열시간을 단축시킬 수 있는 타레소스의 제조방법에 관한 것이다. 본 발명은, 생수 10,000∼12,000cc에 손질된 생닭 700∼1,000g, 사과 400∼680g, 양파 400∼560g, 배 200∼440g, 생강 30∼40g, 대파 330∼480g, 마늘 150∼200g을 넣고 센불로 가열하고 끓기 시작하면 중불로 낮추어 2시간 정도 가열하여 육수를 제조하는 단계; 상기 육수 5,000∼6,000cc를 기준으로 미림 4,500∼5,400cc, 간장 2,500∼3,600cc, 설탕 2,000∼3,000cc를 넣고 육수에 혼합되게 섞어 중간혼합물을 생성하는 단계; 상기 중간혼합물을 가장 센불로 가열하고 끓기 시작하면 중불로 줄어서 3시간 가열하는 단계; 및 가열을 마친 중간혼합물을 상기 중간혼합물이 용기에 담긴 상태로 얼음물에 담가 급냉하는 단계;를 포함한다.

title: Manufacturing method of Dendropanax morbifera tea with improved flavor and blended tea using it
assignee: 이동수
inventors: 이동수
abstract: 본 발명은 풍미를 향상시킨 황칠차 제조방법 및 이를 이용한 블랜딩차에 관한 것으로, 더욱 상세하게는 황칠차의 제조 시, 산패감이 형성되는 것을 방지하며, 풍미와 맛을 향상시킨 형태의 황칠차를 제조하는 방법에 관한 것이다. 본 발명에 따른 풍미를 향상시킨 황칠차 제조방법은, 줄기가 붙어 있는 상태의 황칠잎을 채취하는 제 1 단계, 상기 황칠잎의 수분을 자외선 환경에 노출시켜 수분량을 기설정된 기준에 따라 소량 감소시키는 제 2 단계, 상기 황칠잎을 기설정된 시간만큼 생채기가 생기지 않을 정도의 강도로 흔들어 서로 마찰시키는 제 3 단계, 상기 제 3 단계를 통해 생성된 상기 황칠잎을 덖어주는 제 4 단계, 상기 제 4 단계를 통해 덖어진 상기 황칠잎을 절단하는 제 5 단계, 상기 제 5 단계를 통해 절단된 상기 황칠잎을 비벼 유념하는 제 6 단계, 상기 제 6 단계를 통해 유념된 상기 황칠잎을 건조시키는 제 7 단계를 포함한다. 또한, 풍미를 향상시킨 황칠차를 이용한 블랜딩차는, 상기 황칠차 1 중량부 기준, 1년 이상 발효된 황차를 0.4 내지 0.6 중량부 혼합하여 블랜딩을 실시하는 것을 특징으로 한다. 본 발명에 따른 풍미를 향상시킨 황칠차 제조방법은 황칠잎을 이용하여 풍미와 맛을 향상시킬 수 있고, 차 제조 과정에서 산패 발생 원인을 제거하여 산패감이 없는 황칠차를 제공할 수 있고, 황칠차의 특유의 쌉싸름한 맛을 보완하기 위해, 황차와의 블랜딩을 통해 우수한 차맛을 형성할 수 있는 효과가 있다.

title: ENCAPSULATED APOMORPHINE COMPOSITIONS
assignee: MASSEY VENTURES LTD [NZ]; MASSEY VENTURES LIMITED
inventors: MACKAY SEAN MARSHALL [NZ]; MACKAY, Sean Marshall
abstract: The invention relates to a method of treating a neurological disorder in which an increase in dopaminergic turnover is beneficial, the method comprising intranasal administration of an encapsulated apomorphine composition to a subject in need thereof.

title: ROUTING OF ENCAPSULATED VIDEO STREAMS IN ONE-WAY TRANSFER SYSTEMS
assignee: MICROSOFT TECHNOLOGY LICENSING LLC [US]; Microsoft Technology Licensing, LLC
inventors: WEST JEFFREY ALLEN [US]; SCHULDEN JR WILLIAM HARRY [US]; WEST, Jeffrey Allen et al.
abstract: The present disclosure describes systems and methods relating to full motion video (FMV) routing in one-way transfer (OWT) systems. The present technology enriches datagrams of the video stream that are sent from the low-trust side of the OWT system with a global unique identifier (GUID) that is used as an identifier to determine a particular destination on the high-trust side of the OWT system. The video stream may be encapsulated in a Secure Reliable Transport (SRT) header that includes the unique identifier. When the SRT-wrapped video stream is received on the high-trust side, the GUID in the SRT-header is extracted and used to identify destination addresses for destination devices in the high-trust computing environment. The video stream is then delivered to the destination devices having the corresponding destination addresses.

title: METHOD FOR MANUFACTURING A SYSTEM IN PACKAGE (SIP) USING AN INTEGRATED PACKLET ON LEADFRAME
assignee: ST MICROELECTRONICS INT NV [CH]; STMicroelectronics International N.V.
inventors: RENARD LOIC PIERRE LOUIS [SG]; RENARD, Loic Pierre Louis
abstract: [0000]    A system in package (SiP) includes two or more integrated circuit (IC) packlets performing different functionalities. Each packlet includes a bare functional IC die encapsulated within a first body and including etched individual leads. The packlets are mounted to a leadframe and encapsulated within a second body. A metal heatsink is attached to the leadframe, on a side opposite the two or more IC packlets, using a non-electrically conductive adhesive material.

title: SECURE AND SCALABLE TUNNELING INFRASTRUCTURE FOR DELIVERING HIGH-INTERACTION OS-BASED DECEPTION AS A SERVICE TO LARGE-SCALE IT/OT/IOT MIXED NETWORKS
assignee: FORTINET INC [US]; Fortinet, Inc.
inventors: JIANG JUN [CA]; MI HONGQUAN [CA]; SIMON MOSHE BEN [IL] et al.
abstract: [0000]    Secure data tunnels are established with a plurality of edge points on a plurality of local enterprise networks. At some point, request packets are received in real-time over the first secure tunnel from a first edge point that has encapsulated the request packets. The request packets originate from a malicious actor attacking a projected decoy. Response packets generated from high-fidelity processing of the request packets are encapsulated by the first decoy device and routing to the first secure data tunnel. The response packets are decapsulated and forwarded by edge points back to the malicious actor.

title: LIPID NANOPARTICLES WITH RNA FOR COSMETIC TREATMENT
assignee: LIPOVECTRA INC [US]; Lipovectra Inc.
inventors: BOURGEOIS JASON [US]; LAU FRANK HO PAK [US]; Bourgeois, Jason et al.
abstract: [0000]    A method for directing a change in cell state of white adipose tissue (WAT) which involve treating WAT cells with mRNA encapsulated inside lipid nanoparticles (LNPs). The mRNA encodes UCP1 or other proteins that may transition WAT to brown adipose tissue (BAT). The LNP may contain (C14-4:NCL:Cholesterol:PEG/Lipid Conjugate) at the molar ratio of (35:16:46.5:2.5), (50:10:38.5:1.5) or (56.5:10:31.8:1.7).

title: A FRAMEWORK FOR IMPROVING LOGIC INDUCTION CAPABILITIES IN ARTIFICIAL INTELLIGENCE MODELS
assignee: PAYPAL INC [US]; PAYPAL, INC.
inventors: CHEN ZHE [SG]; XU JUAN [CN]; KANG XIN [CN] et al.
abstract: Methods and systems are presented for providing a framework that improves the logic induction capabilities of an artificial intelligence (AI) model. Under the framework, different logics are encapsulated in a logic knowledge graph. Embeddings are extracted from different portion of the logic knowledge graph, and guiding questions are generated for each logic that is encapsulated within the graph based on the embeddings. A logic database is constructed using the embeddings and the guiding questions. In order for the AI model to perform a task, the logic database is queried to obtain a set of guiding questions corresponding to the task. The guiding questions, along with other information associated with the task, are incorporated into a prompt, which is then provided to the AI model. Based on the guiding questions included in the prompt, the AI model can generate content that follows a particular logic.

title: VERTICAL WETTABLE FLANK FOR A TOP-SIDE PACKAGE
assignee: TEXAS INSTRUMENTS INC [US]; TEXAS INSTRUMENTS INCORPORATED
inventors: AUSTRIA JOMARI [PH]; CLEMENTE LAURA MAY ANTOINETTE D [PH]; SOLAS JEFFREY S [PH] et al.
abstract: A method includes providing an IC package having a lead and a die encapsulated in a mold compound. The mold compound extends from a top mold surface to a base mold surface of the IC package. The method also includes trenching the mold compound from the top mold surface to the lead to form a trench. The method further includes forming a vertical wettable flank by filling the trench with a conductive material.

title: TRANSISTOR THROUGH-HOLE PACKAGE MODULE
assignee: RAYTRONS POWER LTD [CN]; Raytrons Power Limited
inventors: LAM WAI KIN RAYMOND [CN]; LAM, WAI KIN RAYMOND
abstract: [0000]    A transistor through-hole package module includes: an insulating package body and at least one transistor encapsulated therein; a lead frame including through-hole pins extending from the insulating package body and a die pad disposed within the insulating package body. The transistor is mounted on the die pad and electrically connected to the through-hole pins; a metal heat sink plate that is partially exposed from the insulating package body. Both the metal heat sink plate and the lead frame are formed of copper or copper alloy, and the side of the lead frame facing away from the transistor is bonded to the metal heat sink plate via a thermally conductive insulating adhesive. The package module of the present disclosure offers the advantage of superior heat dissipation performance.

title: A METHOD FOR MANUFACTURING A SEMICONDUCTOR POWER PACKAGE AS WELL AS SUCH SEMICONDUCTOR POWER PACKAGE COMPRISING AT LEAST ONE SEMICONDUCTOR DEVICE
assignee: NEXPERIA BV [NL]; NEXPERIA TECH SHANGHAI LTD [CN]; NEXPERIA B.V. et al.
inventors: YUAN ZHIHUI [DE]; GONG WEI [GB]; CAO PULONG [CN] et al.
abstract: A method for manufacturing a semiconductor power package comprising at least one semiconductor device is disclosed, the method comprising the steps of i) providing a lead frame structure having a first lead frame surface and a second lead frame surface opposite to the first lead frame surface, wherein the lead frame structure comprises at least one lead frame die pad and a plurality of lead frame terminals, wherein the at least one lead frame die pad and the plurality of lead frame terminals are at least locally interconnected by means of a plurality of lead frame junctions; ii) mounting at least one semiconductor die having a first die surface and a second die surface opposite to the first die surface, with its first surface in direct contact to a corresponding lead frame die pad; iii) electrically connecting, using one or more connection elements, the at least one semiconductor die with one or more of the plurality of lead terminals; iv) encapsulating, using a mold compound, the lead frame structure, the at least one semiconductor die, the connecting elements and the plurality of lead terminals, such that at least part of the plurality of lead terminals are exposed and thereby forming an encapsulated semiconductor power package; and v) milling, using a milling device, locally through the encapsulating mold compound and through the plurality of lead frame junctions, thereby interrupting the local interconnection between the at least one lead frame die pad and the plurality of lead frame terminals.

title: AROMA-TYPE VENTILATION TUBE
assignee: NINGBO BRIGHTECH INT TRADING CO LTD [CN]; Ningbo Brightech International Trading CO., LTD
inventors: YE JUN [CN]; LU JINQI [CN]; YE, Jun et al.
abstract: The present invention discloses an aroma-type ventilation tube, which may be externally connected to an air source and blows an aroma gas, and a structure of the aroma-type ventilation tube includes a tube body, a ventilation passage arranged inside the tube body, and the like. The tube body mainly includes an aroma base, an aroma cartridge placed in the aroma base, and an aroma replaceably placed in the aroma cartridge. An aroma diffusion hole is provided on the aroma base, and an aroma ventilation hole is provided on the aroma cartridge. In this way, with rotation of the aroma cartridge in the aroma base, the aroma ventilation hole and the aroma diffusion hole are caused to be staggered or overlapped, so that the aroma gas released by the aroma cannot enter the ventilation passage or is released sequentially through the aroma ventilation hole and the aroma diffusion hole to the air passing through the ventilation passage. In addition, the aroma ventilation hole and the aroma diffusion hole can further be used for adjusting, through different overlapping areas, different amounts of the aroma gas entering the ventilation passage. Therefore, a user not only can configure various types of aromas according to actual use requirements, but also can control and adjust release of an aroma gas from the aroma to the air passing through the ventilation passage, thereby meeting use requirements in a plurality of occasions.

title: Trinkflasche
assignee: MAGIC WATER GMBH [CH]; magic water GmbH
inventors: DUDDA PAUL [CH]; GRAUWILER LUKAS [CH]; GIORDANO MILENA [CH] et al.
abstract: Die vorliegende Erfindung betrifft eine Trinkflasche, umfassend einen Behälter für Trinkflüssigkeit, ein Mundstück, einen Trinkflüssigkeits-Transportkanal zum Transport von Trinkflüssigkeit, eine Aromakammer zur Aufnahme eines Aromastoffs, und einen Aroma-Transportkanal zum Transport von mit dem Aromastoff angereicherter Luft, also aromatisierter Luft.Um beim retronasalen Trinken im Vergleich zu den aus dem Stand der Technik bekannten Trinkflaschen eine nutzerfreundlichere, flexiblere und alternative Trinkflasche bereitzustellen, erstreckt sich der Aroma-Transportkanal von der Aromakammer bis zum Mundende des Mundstücks, wobei das Mundstück um eine Mundstück-Drehachse verschwenkbar ausgebildet ist und wobei sich er Trinkflüssigkeits-Transportkanal von dem Mundende des Mundstücks bis in den Behälter erstreckt.

title: TRAVEL JOINT WITH A DUAL ENCAPSULATED COIL STROKE SYSTEM AND METHOD FOR USING THE SAME
assignee: HALLIBURTON ENERGY SERVICES INC [US]; Halliburton Energy Services, Inc.
inventors: LIMA GLAUCIO ROBERTO ALVES DA SILVA [BR]; RODRIGUES JUNIOR DESIDERIO NAEGELE [US]; MEDINA JORGE ARTURO HINOSTROZA [BR] et al.
abstract: [0000]    Disclosed herein are various embodiments of a travel joint having a torque system adapted to attach to a tubular string, a release system attached to the torque system, a dual encapsulated coil stroke system attached to the release system and having an inner mandrel that is surrounded by an inner encapsulated coil and an outer encapsulated coil surrounding the inner encapsulated coil. In some embodiments, different types of control lines may be encapsulated within the inner and outer encapsulated coils.

title: SPIRAL DUCT AND SPIRAL OVEN
assignee: HYC SINGAPORE INC PTE LTD [SG]; HYC (Singapore), Inc. Pte. Ltd.
inventors: NG LIANG SENG [SG]; WONG CHANG CHOY [SG]; TAN JE SEAN [SG] et al.
abstract: The present invention provides a spiral duct, which comprises a plurality of enclosed chambers. Each of the enclosed chambers is assembled sequentially to form a spiral path. A conveyor belt is encapsulated within the plurality of enclosed chambers along the spiral path.

title: BIOMARKER COMPOSITION FOR DIAGNOSING PSORIASIS COMPRISING CLOSTRIDIUM REPTUM STRAIN AS ACTIVE INGREDIENT
assignee: SEOUL NAT UNIV HOSPITAL [KR]; 서울대학교병원
inventors: PARK HYUNSUN [KR]; KO SEONGJOON [KR]; JO SEONG JIN [KR] et al.
abstract: The present invention relates to a biomarker composition for diagnosing psoriasis, comprising a Clostridium leptum strain as an active ingredient. It has been confirmed that the relative intestinal abundance of the strain is significantly reduced in a psoriasis patient group in comparison to that of a normal group, and the strain is effective in alleviating psoriasis symptoms and improving intestinal permeability, and thus can be effectively used as a composition for diagnosing, preventing, treating, or alleviating psoriasis.

title: PHARMACEUTICAL COMPOSITION INCLUDING BETA CORE FRAGMENT HCG AS ACTIVE INGREDIENT
assignee: ADTECH CO LTD [KR]; ADTECH CO., LTD.
inventors: CHANG JIN DONG [KR]; KWAG WON JAE [KR]; CHO SUNG JIN [KR] et al.
abstract: [0000]    The present disclosure relates to a composition for treating infertility, which includes beta core fragment hCG (βcf hCG) as an active ingredient. The βcf hCG is identified to stimulate endometrial development in early pregnancy, is highly expressed in placental tissue, and is involved in maintaining fetal development in early pregnancy. The composition plays an important role in implantation and maintenance of early pregnancy by increasing the thickness of the endometrium, increasing the expression of genes necessary for uterine development, and promoting the growth of uterine-derived cell line. The composition can be usefully used to treat habitual miscarriage in female infertility, and to increase the success rate of pregnancy by improving the implantation rate of fertilized eggs in the process of in vitro fertilization.

title: COMPOSITION COMPRISING CERAMIDE FOR SKIN AND HAIR CARE OF COMPANION ANIMALS
assignee: COSMAX INC [KR]; 코스맥스 주식회사
inventors: CHO HEE YEON [KR]; KIM BO EUN [KR]; PARK EUN JIN [KR] et al.
abstract: The present invention relates to a cosmetic composition comprising a ceramide mixture as an active ingredient for improving the skin and hair condition of companion animals. The composition comprising a ceramide mixture according to one aspect exhibits a skin and hair condition improving effect specific to epidermal cells of the companion dogs due to the synergistic effect resulting from the inclusion of ceramide EOP and ceramide NS at an appropriate weight ratio.

title: POLYOXAZOLINE MICELLAR PHARMACEUTICAL COMPOSITIONS
assignee: THE UNIV OF NORTH CAROLINA AT CHAPEL HILL OFFICE OF TECHNOLOGY COMMERCIALIZATION [US]; DELAQUA PHARMACEUTICALS INC [US]; THE UNIVERSITY OF NORTH CAROLINA AT CHAPEL HILL OFFICE OF TECHNOLOGY COMMERCIALIZATION et al.
inventors: KABANOV ALEXANDER [US]; RAMSEY JACOB [US]; FRANK BRUCE [US] et al.
abstract: Compositions including micelles formed of polyoxazolines are described herein for the effective oral delivery of various active pharmaceutical ingredients (APIs). In one aspect, a composition for oral delivery of an active pharmaceutical ingredient (API) comprises micelles formed of an amphiphilic copolymer including at least one block of hydrophilic poly(2- oxazoline) and at least one block of hydrophobic poly(2-oxazoline), wherein the API and at least one P-glycoprotein (P-gp) inhibitor are contained within the micelles.

title: PHARMACEUTICAL COMPOSITION OF EGFR INHIBITOR AND USE THEREOF
assignee: HONGYUN BIOTECH CO LTD [CN]; 泰州红云制药有限公司
inventors: ZHANG JIANMING [CN]; YUN CAIHONG [CN]; DENG XIANMING [CN] et al.
abstract: The present invention relates to a pharmaceutical composition of an EGFR inhibitor and a use thereof, and specifically provides a use of a combination of a first active ingredient and a second active ingredient in the preparation of a medicament for treating and/or preventing diseases mediated by EGFR kinase. When the first active ingredient is used in combination with the second active ingredient, the complementary advantages of different drugs can be exerted, and resistance can be inhibited while achieving treatment, thereby delaying the occurrence of drug resistance. Meanwhile, a synergistic effect can be produced, tumors can be controlled more effectively, and the therapeutic effect can be enhanced.

title: LIQUID DISCHARGER AND SPRAY CONTAINER
assignee: KAO CORP [JP]; 花王株式会社
inventors: KAWAKAMI TAKERU [JP]; MIHARA KOTA [JP]; 川上　タケル et al.
abstract: This liquid discharger includes a body part capable of pressure-feeding a liquid contained in a container body, and a liquid discharge nozzle for discharging the liquid pressure-fed by the body part. The liquid discharge nozzle has a front wall part provided on a discharge direction side of the liquid discharge nozzle, and a discharge port formed in the front wall part and capable of discharging the liquid. The front wall part has a front surface facing the discharge direction side and a rear surface facing the direction opposite to the discharge direction. The discharge port is formed in a long shape having a major axis and a minor axis, and is provided so as to penetrate from the front surface to the rear surface of the front wall part. In the inner peripheral wall of the discharge port, at least both end parts of the major axis are formed in parallel with the penetration direction of the discharge port, and at least a part of the rear surface of the front wall part constitutes an inclined surface inclined toward the discharge port.

title: SUBSTRATE PROCESSING APPARATUS
assignee: SAMSUNG ELECTRONICS CO LTD [KR]; Samsung Electronics Co., Ltd.
inventors: JI NAYOUNG [KR]; KIM DAIHONG [KR]; KIM IK SOO [KR] et al.
abstract: [0000]    Provided is a substrate processing apparatus including: a chamber providing a process space; a support member disposed in the chamber and configured to support a substrate; and a shower head disposed in the chamber and configured to spray a gas, wherein the shower head includes a central region and an edge spray region disposed around an outer perimeter of the central region, a control gas spray hole is disposed in the central region, and a process gas spray hole is disposed in the edge spray region.

title: BABY WASHER
assignee: JIANG PEIGEN [US]; Jiang Peigen
inventors: JIANG PEIGEN [US]; Jiang, Peigen
abstract: An apparatus for washing baby includes a rigid U-shaped pipe having a first and a second end orienting toward substantially a same direction, a snap coupler disposed at the first end of the U-shaped pipe for removably coupling the first end of the U-shaped pipe to a downward directing snap fitting adapter of a water faucet, and a first spray head capping the second end of the U-shaped pipe, the second end of the U-shaped pipe orienting the first spray head upward.

title: GAS TURBINE ENGINE WITH FUEL DISTRIBUTING COMPRESSOR IMPELLER
assignee: PRATT & WHITNEY CANADA [CA]; PRATT & WHITNEY CANADA CORP.
inventors: MENHEERE DAVID [CA]; HAWIE EDUARDO [CA]; VAN DEN ENDE DANIEL [CA] et al.
abstract: [0000]    A gas turbine engine includes a centrifugal compressor having an impeller and a fuel distributor on a rear face of the impeller. The fuel distributor includes a flange defining a fuel plenum on a radially inner side of the flange, and fuel apertures extending radially through the flange of the fuel distributor. The flange is structure such that rotation of the impeller while the gas turbine engine is operating forces fuel in the fuel plenum to flow radially outward through the fuel apertures to generate a spray of atomized fuel flowing radially outward of the fuel distributor. A combustor located downstream of the centrifugal compressor. A fuel distribution system includes a fuel nozzle having a spray tip located outside of a combustor, the spray tip positioned to inject fuel into the fuel plenum of the fuel distributor on the impeller while the gas turbine engine is operating.

title: SYSTEM AND METHOD FOR CROP SPRAYING USING COMPUTER VISION
assignee: VIVID MACHINES INC [CA]; VIVID MACHINES INC.
inventors: BINAS JONATHAN [CA]; BINAS, Jonathan
abstract: Provided herein are systems and methods of spraying crops. The systems and methods may include, for each given plant of a plurality of plants, capturing an image of the given plant, processing the image to generate a crop metric for the given plant, generating a spraying regime for the given plant based on the crop metric, and transmitting the spraying regimes for the plurality of plants to at least one sprayer configured to spray each of the plurality of plants in accordance with spray regimes.

title: AIR DRYER DEVICE, COMPRESSED AIR SUPPLY SYSTEM, AND VEHICLE
assignee: ZF CV SYSTEMS EUROPE BV [BE]; ZF CV SYSTEMS EUROPE BV
inventors: STABENOW UWE [DE]; MEIER JÖRG [DE]; STABENOW, Uwe et al.
abstract: The invention relates to an air dryer device (LT) for a compressed air supply system (200) of a vehicle (FZ), in particular a passenger car (PN), comprising: a pneumatic compressor connection (1.1) for connecting to a compressor (100); a pneumatic supply connection (2.1) for connecting to a pneumatic main line (15); and an air dryer unit (LE) having a plurality of drying chambers (210) which extend in an axial direction (A) and which each have a drying granulate filling (212). The air dryer device is characterized by a pneumatic switching arrangement (230) which is designed for pneumatically connecting the compressor connection (1.1) at least to a primary drying chamber (210.1) and for pneumatically connecting the supply connection (2.1) selectively at least to the primary drying chamber (210.2) and to a secondary drying chamber (210.2) of the plurality of drying chambers (210), wherein the pneumatic switching arrangement (230) is designed to selectively pneumatically switch the primary drying chamber (210.1) and the secondary drying chamber (210.2) in such a way that, in accordance with operating requirements, at least the primary drying chamber (210.1) can be individually charged with compressed air, and, in accordance with operating requirements, the primary drying chamber (210.1) and the secondary drying chamber (210.2) can be operated such that flow can pass through them sequentially. The invention further relates to a method (1000, 2000) for operating an air dryer device (LT) for a compressed air supply system (200) of a vehicle (FZ) in at least two different operating modes (B1, B2, B3).

title: AIR-DRYER DEVICE, HOUSING COVER FOR AN AIR-DRYER UNIT, COMPRESSED-AIR SUPPLY INSTALLATION AND VEHICLE, AND OPERATING METHOD
assignee: ZF CV SYSTEMS EUROPE BV [BE]; ZF CV SYSTEMS EUROPE BV
inventors: STABENOW UWE [DE]; STABENOW, Uwe
abstract: The invention relates to an air-dryer device (LT) for a compressed-air supply installation (200) of a vehicle (FZ), in particular a passenger car (PN), comprising: - a pneumatic compressor connection (1.1) for connection to a compressor (100), - a pneumatic supply connection (2.1) for connection to a pneumatic main line (12), and - an air-dryer unit (LE) having - a plurality of drying chambers (210) extending in an axial direction (A), each having a desiccant granulate filling (212), and - at least one housing part (220) which is coupled at least to one end face (213, 214) of each of the plurality of drying chambers (210), characterised by - a pneumatic switching arrangement (230) which is designed to pneumatically connect the compressor connection (1.1) at least to a primary drying chamber (210.2) and is designed to pneumatically connect the supply connection (2.1) selectively at least to the primary drying chamber (210.2) and to a secondary drying chamber (210.2) of the plurality of drying chambers (210), and wherein the pneumatic switching arrangement (230) is designed to selectively switch the primary drying chamber (210.1) and the secondary drying chamber (210.2) pneumatically in such a way that, depending on operational demand, at least the primary drying chamber (210.1) can be pressurised individually with compressed air, and, depending on operational demand, the primary drying chamber (210.1) and the secondary drying chamber (210.2) can be operated such that flow can pass through them sequentially. The invention also relates to a method (1000, 2000) for operating an air-dryer device (LT) for a compressed-air supply installation (200) of a vehicle (FZ) in at least two different operating modes (B1, B2, B3).

title: AUTONOMOUS SECURITY ROBOT SYSTEM WITH FLUID-BASED DETERRENT, INK TRACKING, AND AUTOMATED REFILL
assignee: HO CHING TIEN [US]; FONG DAVID [US]; CHIANG REDEM [US] et al.
inventors: HO CHING-TIEN [US]; FONG DAVID [US]; CHIANG REDEM [US] et al.
abstract: A mobile security robot system is disclosed comprising a robot vehicle with pressurized fluid canisters, rotatable spray mechanisms, and a software control system for sensor-based or operator-triggered activation. An integrated invisible ink application module enables covert suspect tagging, while an automated refill interface allows docking with a fluid station for replenishment. The system provides non-lethal deterrence, evidence gathering, and optional environmental uses including plant hydration and wildlife deterrence.

title: DISHWASHER VERTICAL PLUNGER DOOR OPEN SYSTEM
assignee: WHIRLPOOL CO [US]; WHIRLPOOL CORPORATION
inventors: ANNIGERI SHIVAYOGI [IN]; CZARNECKI PHILIP J [US]; GOSHGARIAN DAVID P [US] et al.
abstract: A wax motor is positioned inside the dishwasher. The wax motor moves a strike away from the upper wall of the dishwasher to open the dishwasher door. The wax motor can be activated at the end of a drying cycle to automatically open the door to permit ambient air to assist in drying the dishes and to release humidity that remains in the dishwasher.

title: A METHOD FOR PRODUCING AN ALTERNATIVE CITRUS COMPOSITION AND COMPOSITION THEREOF
assignee: LIN JIAYOU [US]; LIN, Jiayou
inventors: LIN JIAYOU [US]; LIN, Jiayou
abstract: A method of preparing a flavoring composition and composition thereof for imparting citrus juice flavor, taste, and aroma to food stuffs comprising a nano emulsion comprising a mixture of acids, emulsifiers, essential oils, weighting agents, and a thickening and texturizing agent wherein the composition comprises essential oils at 100 to 200 parts per million (ppm); flavor compound mix (selected from a group of recipes) at 50 to 100 ppm, pectin at 0.5 to 1.0 % by weight or 5,000 to 10,000 ppm among other ingredients specific to the type of citrus fruit to be imparted.

title: Pelargonium Plant Named 'KLEPZ23783'
assignee: KLEMM SOHN GMBH & CO KG [DE]; KLEMM + SOHN GMBH & CO. KG
inventors: NEU NINA [DE]; Neu, Nina
abstract: [0000]    A new Pelargonium plant particularly distinguished by purple pink flower color with large red spots on the petals, is disclosed.

title: NUCLEAR POWER PLANT POWER TRADING SYSTEM AND METHOD
assignee: KOREA HYDRO & NUCLEAR POWER CO [KR]; 한국수력원자력 주식회사
inventors: LEE INHOON [KR]; 이인훈
abstract: A nuclear power plant power trading method that links a power exchange and an AI data center, according to an embodiment of the present invention, is characterized by purchasing and supplying computing power through an AI computing power providing platform linking a power exchange server and an AI data center server, and providing surplus power of a nuclear power plant to the AI data center in order to minimize an output reduction operation of the nuclear power plant in connection with the power exchange server and the AI computing power providing platform.

title: PROCESS TO EFFICIENTLY RELEASE SUGARS AND/OR PHENOLIC COMPOUNDS FROM PLANT MATERIAL
assignee: UNIV GENT [BE]; UNIVERSITEIT GENT
inventors: RAES KATLEEN [BE]; STAMOGIANNOU IOANNA [BE]; RAES, Katleen et al.
abstract: The present invention relates to methods that are useful to recuperate bioactive compounds that are present in plant-based biomass. More speciﬁcally, the present invention discloses eƯicient methods to extract phenolic compounds and sugars from plant material.

title: ZEOLITE BASED SOIL COMPOSITION FOR AGRICULTURAL AND HORTICULTURAL PLANT GROWTH
assignee: WATAD AGRO COMPANY [JO]; WATAD AGRO COMPANY
inventors: AL-MANASIR MOHANNAD TAYSEER SALEM [JO]; AL-MANASIR, Mohannad Tayseer Salem
abstract: The present disclosure provides a soil composition including zeolites, mineral components, activating components, and additives, wherein zeolites may include phillipsite, chabazite, and faujasite. Furthermore, mineral components may include brown tuff, coarse tuff, olivine, clay, and pyroxene, while the activating components may include calcite, smectite, volcanic glass, and feldspar. On the other hand, beneficial additives and fertilizers related additives are included in the composition of the present disclosure to improve soil health and nutrient availability. The soil composition is configured to improve plant growth and optimizing soil health for sustainable agricultural and horticultural applications. The present disclosure also provides a method for preparing the soil composition.

title: GROUND-BASED TRANSPORT APPARATUS AND LOGISTICS PROCESSING SYSTEM INCLUDING THE SAME
assignee: SAMSUNG ELECTRONICS CO LTD [KR]; SEMES CO LTD [KR]; SAMSUNG ELECTRONICS CO., LTD. et al.
inventors: BAEK GYEONG DAM [KR]; YOON JI WON [KR]; SON WANG HYEON [KR] et al.
abstract: A ground-based transport apparatus for transporting a container in a semiconductor manufacturing plant includes a driving controller configured to move the ground-based transport apparatus on a ground of the semiconductor manufacturing plant, a housing on the driving controller, a shelf structure in the housing and including a plurality of shelves, and a manipulator arm assembly in the housing and spaced apart from the shelf structure. The manipulator arm assembly is configured to remove the container from a first shelf of the plurality of shelves, and set the container on a second shelf of the plurality of shelves. The driving controller is further configured to transport the container between a plurality of facilities, and store the container in a ground-based storage apparatus disposed on the ground of the semiconductor manufacturing plant. Each facility of the plurality of facilities includes a substrate treating apparatus. The container stores a substrate.

title: NANOPARTICLE DETECTION WITH INTEGRATED NANOPARTICLE COMPOSITION DETERMINATION
assignee: ELEMENTAL SCIENTIFIC INC [US]; Elemental Scientific, Inc.
inventors: KIM MYUNG HWAN [US]; WIEDERIN DANIEL R [US]; SCHULTZ AUSTIN [US] et al.
abstract: Systems and methods for analyzing fluid samples containing nanoparticles for the determination of nanoparticle count and nanoparticle elemental composition are described. In an aspect, a system embodiment includes, but is not limited to, a remote sampling system configured to receive a fluid sample containing nanoparticles, the remote sampling system including a particle counter configured to determine a number of nanoparticles present in the fluid sample via light scattering detection; a sample analysis system configured to receive the fluid sample from the remote sampling system, the sample analysis system including a mass spectrometer configured to determine an elemental composition of the nanoparticles present in the fluid sample; and a system controller communicatively configured to provide a distribution of the nanoparticles in the fluid sample by specific element type based on an output from each of the mass spectrometer and the particle counter.

title: MODIFYING TRANSPLANTABLE ORGANS EX VIVO WITH RNA LIPID NANOPARTICLE PERFUSION
assignee: VERSITI BLOOD RES INSTITUTE FOUNDATION INC [US]; VERSITI BLOOD RESEARCH INSTITUTE FOUNDATION, INC.
inventors: BADIOR KATHERINE [US]; TURNER HALEN [US]; KASTRUP CHRISTIAN [US] et al.
abstract: The present invention provides methods for modifying a donor organ, e.g., liver, or a portion thereof. The methods comprise contacting the donor liver with lipid nanoparticles comprising RNA. Compositions of lipid nanoparticles comprising RNA are also provided. The disclosed LNPs may comprise an siRNA, e.g., targeting tissue plasminogen activator (tPA) or plasminogen activator inhibitor 1 (PAI-1). The LNPs may comprise an mRNA encoding a protein, e.g., Nuclear Factor-erythroid factor 2-related factor 2 (NRF2) or thrombomodulin.

title: STENT DELIVERY SYSTEMS
assignee: BOSTON SCIENT SCIMED INC [US]; BOSTON SCIENTIFIC SCIMED, INC.
inventors: STULL AMANDA GRACE [US]; LOGAN BRADY SCOTT [US]; NAGEL MICHAEL WILLIAM [US] et al.
abstract: Stent delivery systems as well as methods for making and using stent delivery systems are disclosed. An example stent delivery system may include an elongate shaft including an inner member having a stent receiving region, a deployment sheath slidably disposed along the inner member, and an intermediate shaft disposed between the inner member and the deployment sheath. A cuff may be coupled to the deployment sheath. The deployment sheath may include a floating braid region mechanically engaged with the cuff.

title: ATTACHABLE DELIVERY DEVICES AND METHODS FOR ATTACHING THE ATTACHABLE DELIVERY DEVICE TO A TARGET
assignee: NORTHROP GRUMMAN SYSTEMS CORP [US]; Northrop Grumman Systems Corporation
inventors: OJA KYLE [US]; GARBE DUANE J [US]; CHRISTENSEN JUSTIN [US] et al.
abstract: [0000]    An attachable delivery device is configured to be carried by a delivery vehicle and to deploy a payload at a target. The attachable delivery device includes a base configured to house the payload. The attachable delivery device also includes at least one tape spring attached to the base. The at least one tape spring is configured to deploy from a first transport position to a second deployed position. The attachable delivery device also includes a buckle contact configured to attach to the base and to move relative to the base. The movement of the buckle contact relative to the base is configured to cause the at least one tape spring to deploy from the first transport position to the second deployed position.

title: DRUG DELIVERY DEVICE AND RELATED METHODS
assignee: AMGEN INC [US]; AMGEN INC.
inventors: EILERTSEN LARS [US]; EILERTSEN, Lars
abstract: A drug delivery device may include a housing having a distal end, a proximal end, and an opening adjacent to the distal end, a flange protruding radially inward from an inner surface of the housing, a drug storage container including a delivery member having an insertion end configured to extend at least partially through the opening, a plunger moveable in a distal direction to expel a drug from the drug storage container through the delivery member, a guard moveably positioned adjacent to the opening, a guard extension positioned proximal to the guard and operably coupled to the guard, a releaser member operably coupled to the guard extension, the releaser member having a distally facing contact surface, and a first biasing member disposed between the flange of the housing and the distally facing contact surface of the releaser member. The first biasing member may be configured to bias the releaser member in a proximal direction in at least a pre-delivery state.

title: PVC COMPOUND
assignee: YE JIANTAO [US]; YE JINTIAN [US]; YE ALEC [US] et al.
inventors: YE JIANTAO [US]; YE JINTIAN [US]; YE ALEC [US] et al.
abstract: A PVC compound has by weight: 60% chlorinated polyvinyl chloride (CPVC); 15% unplasticized polyvinyl chloride (uPVC); 1.5% a first acrylic resin compound (ACR); 6% calcium carbonate (CaCO3); 1.8% an organotin stabilizer; 0.18% organic peroxide (OPE); 0.36% a process aid; 0.73% a lubricant, wherein the lubricant acts as a blowing agent; 10.8% chlorinated polyethylene (CPE); and 3% a second acrylic resin compound (ACR). The PVC has a Vicat softening temperature of the compound of 200° F. or higher. The compound is used as a substrate in co-extruded PVC profiles for windows and doors or extruded for applications including fences, decks, and siding. The CPE and ACR components enhance the impact resistance of the compound. The process aid is an internal lubricant such as a decanedioic acid oleic alcohol ester or stearyl phthalate. The blowing agent is a mineral oil.

title: NANOPARTICLE FORMULATIONS FOR THE DELIVERY OF DNA TO ADIPOSE TISSUE
assignee: REMEDIUM BIO INC [US]; HOLLANDER JUDITH [US]; SEREGIN ALEXEY [US] et al.
inventors: HOLLANDER JUDITH [US]; SEREGIN ALEXEY [US]; GORALTCHOUK ALEXEI [US] et al.
abstract: The present invention generally relates at least in part to gene delivery vehicles, and more specifically, in some embodiments, DNA delivery vehicles for the treatment of human or veterinary conditions.

title: PERSONAL CARE COMPOSITION BASED ON CARBOXYMETHYL CYSTEINE COMPOUND AND ACETYL METHIONINE COMPOUND
assignee: UNILEVER IP HOLDINGS B V [NL]; UNILEVER GLOBAL IP LTD [GB]; CONOPCO INC DBA UNILEVER [US] et al.
inventors: GU XUELAN [NL]; XIAO XUE [NL]; GU, Xuelan et al.
abstract: Disclosed is a personal care composition comprising carboxymethyl cysteine compound and acetyl methionine compound, wherein the molar ratio of the carboxymethyl cysteine compound to the acetyl methionine compound is 1:1.6 to 2:1.

title: COMPOUND EXPANSION ENGINE
assignee: BENHAM ROGER A [US]; BENHAM, Roger, A.
inventors: BENHAM ROGER [US]; BENHAM, Roger
abstract: An improved reciprocating engine with compound expansion suitable for power generation and in particular a reciprocating engine with compound, or secondary, expansion, especially suited for the direct injection of fuel and oxidizer for higher efficiency, carbon sequestration, and Nox reduction in large displacement combustion engines.

title: DELIVERY SYSTEMS FOR CONFORMABLE FILLABLE MEDICAL BALLOONS
assignee: BOSTON SCIENT SCIMED INC [US]; BOSTON SCIENTIFIC MEDICAL DEVICE LIMITED [IE]; Boston Scientific Scimed, Inc. et al.
inventors: CLEVELAND BENJAMIN [US]; KNOWLES KATHERINE ANN POLLACK [US]; PARISI CRISTIAN [US] et al.
abstract: In some aspects, the disclosure provides systems and methods for delivering a conformable, fillable balloon to a target location. A balloon delivery system and balloon may include a tubular elongate shaft with a first lumen extending from a proximal end to a distal end and a hypotube with a second lumen extending from a proximal end to a distal end. A conformable, fillable balloon may be releasably coupled to the distal end of the elongate shaft or hypotube. The balloon may be expandable from a compressed lower profile delivery configuration to an expanded deployed configuration. An interior cavity of the balloon may be in fluid communication with the lumen of the elongate shaft. The balloon is configured to contain a filler material and bound the spread of the filler material as it gels or hardens.

title: INSULIN DELIVERY MANAGEMENT IN CLOSED-LOOP SYSTEMS
assignee: DIABELOOP [FR]; DIABELOOP
inventors: ROUSSON SYLVAIN [FR]; ABISSET HOEL [FR]; HUNEKER ERIK [FR] et al.
abstract: A control device for managing insulin delivery in a closed-loop system. The control device comprises a computation module. The computation module is configured to receive a target basal rate indicative of a rate of a target insulin delivery rate of an insulin pump and compute a supplemental basal rate if the target basal rate is greater than a rate threshold. The supplemental basal rate is sensibly equal to a difference between the target basal rate and the rate threshold. The control device also comprises an insulin management module. The insulin management module is configured to determine a recommendation value of a control parameter of the insulin pump. The recommendation value is based on the supplemental basal rate multiplied by a time period. The control device comprises a communication module. The communication module is configured to send the recommendation value to the insulin pump.

title: PERSONAL CARE COMPOSITION BASED ON AN ACETYL AMINOACID COMPOUND AND A CYSTINE COMPOUND
assignee: UNILEVER IP HOLDINGS B V [NL]; UNILEVER GLOBAL IP LTD [GB]; CONOPCO INC DBA UNILEVER [US] et al.
inventors: GU XUELAN [NL]; GU, Xuelan
abstract: Disclosed is a personal care composition comprising acetyl amino acid compound and cystine compound, wherein the weight ratio of acetyl amino acid compound to cystine compound is 5:1 to 16:1.

title: INTELLIGENT VOLATILE ORGANIC COMPOUND SEQUESTERING
assignee: IBM [US]; International Business Machines Corporation
inventors: MORONES REBECCA N [US]; WERNER JOHN S [US]; TSFASMAN ARKADIY O [US] et al.
abstract: [0000]    A computer-implemented method that reduces volatile organic compounds in an atmosphere is provided. A number of processor units extract a physical layout of a data center from a database. A number of volatile organic compound sensors captures a set of volatile organic compound data within the data center. Responsive to the capturing, the number of processor units creates a volatile organic compound map based on the physical layout and the set of volatile organic compound data. The number of processor units reposition a mobile volatile organic compound sequestering device to one or more locations in the data center based on the volatile organic compound map and the physical layout. According to other illustrative embodiments, a computer system and computer program product for reducing volatile organic compounds in an atmosphere are provided.

title: SYSTEMS AND METHODS FOR FUEL STORAGE AND DELIVERY
assignee: TIBERIUS AEROSPACE INC [US]; TIBERIUS AEROSPACE, INC.
inventors: STEELBERG CHAD [US]; STEELBERG, Chad
abstract: The present disclosure provides a pressurized fuel storage and delivery system comprising a tank with interior surfaces defining a fuel storage volume, a flexible bladder within the tank configured to contain fuel, a gas reservoir within the tank configured to store pressurized gas, a micro-perforated separator between the gas reservoir and bladder configured to allow controlled gas flow to a space surrounding the bladder. In some cases, microchannels on the tank interior surfaces or bladder exterior surface are configured to distribute pressurized gas around the bladder to control compression patterns and manage thermal effects during gas expansion. The system further includes pressurization port connected to the gas reservoir configured to introduce pressurized gas, and a fuel port connected to the bladder configured to allow fuel delivery when pressurized gas compresses the bladder.

title: PROSTHETIC VALVE DELIVERY SYSTEM
assignee: MEDTRONIC INC [US]; MEDTRONIC, INC.
inventors: TRICARICO ROSAMARIA [US]; WU PEI-CHI [US]; PATEL MANTHAN P [US] et al.
abstract: A delivery system includes an outer shaft assembly including an outer shaft and an outer distal hinge component, an inner shaft assembly including an inner shaft and an inner shaft distal hinge component, the inner shaft slidingly and rotationally disposed within the outer shaft, and a capsule shaft assembly including a capsule shaft and a capsule coupled to the capsule shaft, the capsule shaft slidingly and rotationally disposed within the inner shaft, the capsule configured to receive a prosthetic heart valve therein. The capsule shaft assembly is configured to be selectively rotated and longitudinally translated independent of the inner shaft assembly and the outer shaft assembly. The inner shaft assembly is configured to be selectively rotated and longitudinally translated independent of the capsule shaft assembly and the outer shaft assembly. The inner distal hinge component and the outer distal hinge component are each configured to pivot independently.

title: OXOCARBON COMPOUND
assignee: NIPPON CATALYTIC CHEM IND [JP]; 株式会社日本触媒
inventors: NIMURA SHIMPEI [JP]; HORIOKA AMI [JP]; AOKI MASANORI [JP] et al.
abstract: Provided is an oxocarbon compound represented by formula (1) or formula (2). In formula (3), ring A represents a 10- to 18-membered unsaturated hydrocarbon ring, ring B represents an aromatic hydrocarbon ring, an aromatic heterocyclic ring, or a condensed ring including the ring structure of these rings, ring A and ring B may have a substituent, X represents an alkyl group, an aryl group, or an aralkyl group, and * represents a bonding site with the 4-membered ring in formula (1) or the 5-membered ring in formula (2).

title: COMPOSITION FOR THE PREPARATION OF ALCOHOLIC BEVERAGES BASED ON Heliopsis longipes AND ITS MANUFACTURING PROCESS
assignee: TORRES LANDA ARANDA MARIANA [MX]; Torres Landa Aranda Mariana
inventors: TORRES LANDA ARANDA MARIANA [MX]; Torres Landa Aranda, Mariana
abstract: A formulation made from Heliopsis longipes, a perennial plant commonly known as chilcuague. The plant presents a high concentration of bioactive compounds in its root, which can be effectively utilized when extracted using appropriate solvents such as ethanol. In contrast, aqueous infusions yield low detectable levels of such compounds. The extraction process significantly influences the physicochemical parameters of the resulting solution, particularly its color and, to a lesser extent, its pH. This provides an opportunity to develop food products that are regularly consumed while incorporating high levels of bioactive extracts into their formulations. The process includes the steps of immersion cleaning, hydration, bark tearing, maceration, and preservation—Heliopsis longipes root transfers its organoleptic and functional properties to an alcoholic vector, producing an innovative composition for the elaboration of alcoholic beverages such as tequila, rum, vodka, gin, and others.

title: COMPOSITION FOR REMOVING EDGE BEADS FROM METAL-CONTAINING RESISTS, DEVELOPER COMPOSITION OF METAL-CONTAINING RESISTS, AND METHOD OF FORMING PATTERNS USING THE COMPOSITION
assignee: SAMSUNG SDI CO LTD [KR]; SAMSUNG SDI CO., LTD.
inventors: KWAK TAEKSOO [KR]; MOON HYUNGRANG [KR]; HO JONGPIL [KR] et al.
abstract: A composition to remove edge beads from a metal-containing resist or a developer composition of a metal-containing resist and a method of forming or providing patterns utilizing the composition and/or the developer composition are disclosed. The composition to remove edge beads from the metal-containing resist or the developer composition of the metal-containing resist may include a polyphenol-based compound; and an organic solvent, wherein the polyphenol-based compound is in an amount of about 10 wt % to about 50 wt % based on 100 wt % of the composition or the developer composition.

title: A COMPOSITE MATERIAL, IN PARTICULAR LAMINATED COMPOSITE MATERIAL
assignee: COMPOSITE RES S R L [IT]; COMPOSITE RESEARCH S.R.L.
inventors: FOSSAT EUGENIO [IT]; GIULIETTI NICOLA [IT]; QUERO ALESSANDRA [IT] et al.
abstract: Disclosed herein is a composite material (1) comprising a polymer matrix (2) and fibres (4) arranged in said polymer matrix (2), wherein: - the polymer matrix (2) comprises a polyphenol in an amount equal to or greater than 15% by weight with respect to the weight of the polymer matrix (2), the fibres (4) have aa tensile modulus of elasticity, measured according to ASTM C1557, greater than 50 GPa, and are present in an amount equal to or greater than 80% by weight with respect to the weight of the polymer matrix (2).

title: METHOD AND FORMULATION FOR IMPROVED SHELF-STABILITY OF STARCH-BASED FOODS USING POLYPHENOLS
assignee: THE STARDUST FOOD COMPANY [US]; The Stardust Food Company
inventors: SHINO MIKA [US]; HANSEN KJETIL [US]; Shino, Mika et al.
abstract: A method and formulation for improvement in at least the texture and shelf stability of starch-based foods is disclosed, such as but not limited to gummy candies, through the incorporation of polyphenols, wherein said polyphenols may include but are not limited to one or more of flavonoids, phenolic acids, lignans, stilbenes, and tannins. This method and formulation comprises the use of polyphenol molecules to improve and stabilize starch-based foods for extended periods of time without the use of artificial preservatives. The method and formulation including one or more polyphenols also allows utilization of specific beneficial qualities of the polyphenols including: flavor, aroma, color and the numerous associated health benefits. Thus, the method and formulation facilitates the production of various starch-based foods (e.g., chewy candies, mochi, breads, cakes, pastas, etc.) with unique and previously unattainable qualities that are advantageous for consumer-packaged foods.

title: Reduced browning in banana fruit
assignee: TROPIC BIOSCIENCES UK LTD [GB]; Tropic Biosciences UK Limited
inventors: DAVID GUILLAUME-SCHOEPFER [GB]; CRISTINA PIGNOCCHI [GB]; David Guillaume-Schoepfer et al.
abstract: A banana plant cell comprising three copies of a Polyphenol oxidase 1 (PPO1) gene, wherein a first copy comprises a premature stop codon TGA in a nucleotide sequence comprising TGACCT, a second copy comprises a premature stop codon TAG in a nucleotide sequence comprising TAGGCA, and a third copy comprises a premature stop codon TGA in a nucleotide sequence comprising TGAGGG. The PPO1 genes can comprise an indel causing a frameshift in the wildtype PPO1 gene sequence leading to each premature stop codon. The indel can be at polynucleotide sequences GAGACCA and ACCCGTC of the PPO1 gene. The indel can be an insertion or a deletion. The first PPO1 gene can encode a polypeptide of about 95 to 100 amino acids in length. The second PPO1 gene can encode a polypeptide of about 65 to 70 amino acids in length. The third PPO1 gene can encode a polypeptide of about 130 to 135 amino acids in length. A further aspect is a banana, a banana fruit, and a processed banana product comprising the banana plant cell. The banana can be autotriploid, preferably Musa acuminata. No figure

title: ENZYMATIC MODIFICATION OF RAPESEED MEAL EXTRACTED POLYPHENOLS AND APPLICATION OF ANTIOXIDATIVE AND PREBIOTICALLY ACTIVE MODIFIED EXTRACTS IN FOOD PRODUCTS
assignee: TEHNOLOSKO METALURSKI FAKULTET UNIVERZITETA U BEOGRADU [RS]; TEHNOLOŠKO-METALURŠKI FAKULTET UNIVERZITETA U BEOGRADU
inventors: SIMOVIĆ MILICA [RS]; BANJANAC KATARINA [RS]; VELJKOVIĆ MILICA [RS] et al.
abstract: The present invention refers to a two-step process of extraction using 70% ethanol anc subsequent enzymatic modification of polyphenol from rapeseed meal, and the application of the obtained modified extract with prebiotic activity in food products to obtain a functiona food product that has potential therapeutic effects in treating gut diseases caused by an imbalance of E. coli and S. boulardii, such as diarrhea, abdominal pain, and inflammator) bowel disease. By using a commercial enzyme preparation under mild conditions in a system without organic solvents, an extract with altered polyphenol profile, unchanged antioxidant capacity and more pronounced prebiotic activity towards these two bacterial species comparec to an extract obtained using the conventional extraction process with 70% ethanol. The food product (apple juice without added sugar) containing the target extract exhibits a positive effect on the growth of S. boulardii, while it has a highly inhibitory effect on the growth of the pathogenic E. coli strain, making this functional food product potentially therapeutic for treating gut diseases caused by gut microbiota imbalance.

title: FORMULATION FOR IRON CHELATION THERAPY AND METHOD OF PREPARATION THEREOF
assignee: BIRLA INSTITUTE OF TECH & SCIENCE BITS PILANI [IN]; BIRLA INSTITUTE OF TECHNOLOGY & SCIENCE (BITS), PILANI
inventors: DUTTA JAYATI RAY [IN]; GANESAN RAMAKRISHNAN [IN]; TATA PRANATHI [IN] et al.
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a formulation for iron chelation therapy comprising: a water-soluble polyphenol-biogenic amine complex comprising of a polyphenol and an amine, and a 0.9% of sodium chloride; optionally one or more additive, wherein water-soluble polyphenol-biogenic amine complex effectively chelates the excess iron and enable its elimination. The present disclosure also provides a method of preparation of a formulation for iron chelation therapy comprising: a) adding 0.005 to 5% w/w of a polyphenol and 0.005 to 5% w/w of an amine followed by adjusting to pH-7 by adding HCl/NaHCO3 to form a mixture; and b) mixing the mixture of step a) with suitable proportion in 0.9% sodium chloride and optionally one or more additive to form a formulation for iron chelation therapy, wherein water-soluble polyphenol-biogenic amine complex effectively chelates the excess iron and enable its elimination.

title: MICROENCAPSULATION OF RUBBER- AND POLYMER-MODIFIED BITUMEN FOR STORAGE AND TRANSPORTATION
assignee: TEXAS A & M UNIV SYS [US]; PUMA ENERGY SUPPLY & TRADING PTE LTD [AU]; THE TEXAS A&M UNIVERSITY SYSTEM et al.
inventors: ANITA FNU [US]; DENNEMAN ERIK [AU]; BANERJEE SARBAJIT [US] et al.
abstract: [0000]    The subject invention pertains to compositions and methods of encapsulating bitumen for storage and transportation. More specifically, the capsule surrounding bitumen can contain electrolyte layers, bound through electrostatic interactions, on the surface of bitumen.

title: NOVEL COMPLEX DERIVED FROM NATURAL EXTRACT HAVING PH ADJUSTED BY ACID-BASE REACTION AND METHOD FOR SYNTHESIZING SAME
assignee: ROOTREN INC [KR]; 주식회사 루트렌
inventors: SEO GANGMIN [KR]; 서강민
abstract: The present invention relates to a novel complex derived from a natural extract having pH adjusted by an acid-base reaction, and a method for synthesizing same. The present invention provides a novel complex synthesized by chemical reaction formula 1. [Chemical reaction formula 1] Acid + (base + polyphenol-containing natural extract) → novel complex + by-product

title: SERUM-PROTEIN COATED FUSOGENIC LIPID PARTICLES FOR TARGETED DRUG DELIVERY
assignee: RE3B THERAPEUTICS UG I G [DE]; RE3B THERAPEUTICS UG (I.G.)
inventors: CSISZÁR AGNES [DE]; HOFFMANN BERND [DE]; HERSCH NILS [DE] et al.
abstract: The invention is based on the finding that fusogenic lipid particles, when coated with serum proteins such as apolipoproteins, retain their fusogenic properties. By coating fusogenic particles with serum proteins such as apolipoproteins, the inventors provide protein-coated fusogenic lipid particles that can be used for targeted drug delivery. The invention pertains to said protein-coated fusogenic lipid particles, their method of manufacturing and therapeutic use, und to methods for targeted delivery of drugs and of the fusogenic lipid particles that rely on the coating of the fusogenic lipid particles with serum proteins. In particular embodiments, the protein-coated fusogenic particles of the invention comprise a polyphenol.

title: POLYPHENOL OXIDASE, AN AMINO ACID TRANSPORTER, AND A TRANSGENIC CELL, TISSUE, AND ORGANISM COMPRISING SAME
assignee: YEDA RES & DEV [IL]; YEDA RESEARCH AND DEVELOPMENT CO. LTD.
inventors: AHARONI ASAPH [IL]; VAINER ANDRII [IL]; BERMAN PAULA [IL] et al.
abstract: The present invention provides isolated DNA molecule(s), isolated polypeptide(s), artificial vector(s), and cell(s) including same. Further provided are methods for increasing the amount of an amino acid, as well as for synthesizing L-DOPA including a derivative thereof.

title: Nanoparticle system comprising a metal coated with polyethylene glycol (PEG), which is functionalized with a polyphenol-type organic acid, manufacturing process, and use of said system (Machine-translation by Google Translate, not legally binding)
assignee: UNIV ROVIRA I VIRGILI [ES]; UNIV ALCALA HENARES [ES]; Universitat Rovira i Virgili et al.
inventors: DE LA MATA DE LA MATA FRANCISCO JAVIER [ES]; ORTEGA LOPEZ PAULA [ES]; BOUAOUZ IDRISSI SIHAM [ES] et al.
abstract: Nanoparticle system comprising a metal coated with polyethylene glycol (PEG) functionalized with a polyphenolic organic acid, manufacturing process, and use of said system. The present invention relates to a nanoparticle system comprising a metal selected from silver and gold, coated with polyethylene glycol (PEG), functionalized with a polyphenolic organic acid selected from caffeic acid, gallic acid, and ferulic acid. The present invention also relates to a manufacturing process for said nanoparticle system and to the therapeutic and cosmetic use of said nanoparticle system. title: HONEY PRODUCTS CONTAINING FRUIT- AND VEGETABLE POWDERS
assignee: NATUR SZ KFT [HU]; NATUR-SZ Kft.
inventors: DR CZUKOR BÁLINT [HU]; SZUHÁNYI ISTVÁNNÉ [HU]; dr. Czukor Bálint et al.
abstract: The invention relates to new honey products containing fruit and/or vegetable powders with an increased polyphenol content and a minimum shelf life of 1 year and to their production. The product comprises 2-20% by weight of fruit and/or vegetable powders with a moisture content of 2-20% by weight and a particle size of 0.2-0.8 mm. The powders are obtained from naturally polyphenol-containing fruit and/or vegetable juices. The product optionally contains 2-20% by weight of maltodextrin or modified starch additives. The fruit and/or vegetable powders are uniformly mixed with honey, in an amount of 2-20% by weight of the product.

title: Peony polyphenol extract as well as preparation method and application thereof
assignee: INST BOTANY JIANGSU PROVINCE & CAS; JIANGSU PEONY TECH CO LTD; 江苏省中国科学院植物研究所 et al.
inventors: SHAN YU; ZHAO XINRUI; WANG QIZHI et al.
abstract: The invention discloses a peony polyphenol extract as well as a preparation method and application thereof, and relates to the technical field of biological medicines. Dried flowers of paeonia ostii are used as raw materials, peony polyphenol powder is obtained through an ethanol extraction-pore resin purification method, and the polyphenol content of the peony polyphenol powder is larger than 80% through determination of a folin-phenol method. Paeonia suffruticosa polyphenol is rich in more than 30 kinds of main active ingredients, and comprises flavonoids, phenolic acids and polyphenol derivatives, and flavonoids and phenolic acid compounds account for 81.25% of the total number of identified ingredients. The peony polyphenol plays a role in resisting cervical cancer through double ways of inducing HeLa cell apoptosis and activating HeLa cell autophagy, and the problem of drug resistance of a single-target drug can be effectively solved; the compound has a good application value in anti-cancer drugs. Meanwhile, the peony polyphenol shows excellent antioxidant activity in various free radical models, remarkably inhibits ROS (reactive oxygen species) generation at a cellular level, relieves oxidative stress, has a cell protection effect, and can be used as a natural antioxidant and a cell protection agent.

title: Preparation method of polyphenol-polysaccharide synergistically-improved thermally-stable fish gelatin gel and 3D printing application of polyphenol-polysaccharide synergistically-improved thermally-stable fish gelatin gel
assignee: UNIV DALIAN POLYTECHNIC; 大连工业大学
inventors: DONG XIUPING; ZHOU MENGDI; WANG YUE et al.
abstract: The invention discloses a preparation method of polyphenol-polysaccharide synergistically-improved thermally-stable fish gelatin gel and 3D printing application of the polyphenol-polysaccharide synergistically-improved thermally-stable fish gelatin gel. According to the method, tannic acid and sodium carboxymethyl cellulose are introduced into a fish gelatin system and are fully stirred and mixed, and tannic acid as a cross-linking agent interacts with sodium carboxymethyl cellulose and fish gelatin to construct a more compact three-dimensional gel network, so that the preparation of the polyphenol and polysaccharide synergistically enhanced fish gelatin-based gel is realized. The gel shows excellent printing adaptability and structural stability in 3D printing application, geometric shapes can be accurately formed, the gel has wide application prospects in the fields of personalized food design, biological material manufacturing and the like, and a new technical path is provided for high-value development of fish gelatin.

title: Lemon preservation method based on calcium-salicylic acid and tea polyphenol-chitosan
assignee: GUIZHOU FRUIT INSTITUTE GUIZHOU CITRUS RESEARCH INSTITUTE GUIZHOU SPECIAL FRUIT AND VEGETABLE ENGINE; 贵州省果树科学研究所（贵州省柑橘研究所、贵州省特色果蔬工程技术中心、贵州省火龙果研究所）
inventors: FANG XIAOTONG; WANG QIANJU; WANG YU et al.
abstract: The invention discloses a lemon fresh-keeping method based on calcium-salicylic acid and tea polyphenol-chitosan. The lemon fresh-keeping method comprises the steps of pretreatment before picking, soaking treatment after picking and storage management. 14 days before picking, a compound solution of 0.23 g/L chelated calcium and 2.0 mmol/L salicylic acid is sprayed to lemon fruits; after harvesting, the fruits are soaked in a 2% tea polyphenol and 1% chitosan composite fresh-keeping solution for 3 min, and the fruits are stored at the normal temperature of 25 DEG C after being dried in the shade; through the synergistic treatment, the hardness increase, weight loss rate increase and color deterioration of the lemon fruits can be obviously delayed, relatively high contents of total acids, total phenols, ascorbic acid and flavonoids are maintained, the accumulation of malonaldehyde is inhibited, and the activity of superoxide dismutase, peroxidase and catalase is improved; after the fruits are stored for 40 days, the weight loss rate of the fruits is only 30.37%, the total phenol content is 0.486 mg/mL, the SOD activity is 46.63 U/mL, and the fresh-keeping effect is obviously superior to that of a single treatment group and a control group; the method is simple in process, controllable in cost and high in safety.

title: DRUG IMPLANT DELIVERY DEVICE
assignee: CONGRUENCE MEDICAL SOLUTIONS LLC [US]; SCHAEFER ANDREW [US]; EINFELD LANCE [US] et al.
inventors: SCHAEFER ANDREW [US]; EINFELD LANCE [US]; CASTAGNA LOU [US] et al.
abstract: A drug implant delivery device includes a needle; an implant guide comprising a channel that is coaxial with the needle and configured to receive a drug implant; a gate configured to releasably limit the drug implant from moving into the needle; a barrel; a spring-loaded assembly comprising an elastomeric component received in the barrel and a stylet extending coaxially with the channel; and an actuator comprising: a first arm operably coupled to the gate, and a second arm releasably engaged with the spring-loaded assembly, wherein the actuator is configured such that actuation of the actuator causes the first arm to move the gate such that the drug implant can be moved into the needle and the second arm to disengage from the spring-loaded assembly such that the stylet translates to move the drug implant through the needle and into an injection site.

title: APPARATUS FOR PROCESSING WORKPIECE AND METHOD FOR ARRANGING BEAM DELIVERY SYSTEM IN PROCESSING UNIT
assignee: ORC MFG CO LTD [JP]; ORC MANUFACTURING CO., LTD.
inventors: FUNAYAMA MASAHIKO [JP]; SHIMIZU SHUICHI [JP]; WASHIYAMA HIROYUKI [JP] et al.
abstract: [0000]    An apparatus for processing a workpiece includes a body having a supporting structure, a beam-delivery system configured to transmit a beam from a light source to an illumination optical unit, and a vibration isolation unit configured to support the supporting structure. The supporting structure supports the illumination optical unit and a processing stage. A workpiece is mountable on the processing stage. The light source is positioned separately from the body. The beam-delivery system includes a body-side beam delivery unit supported by the supporting structure. The body-side beam delivery unit includes a correcting optical member configured to adjust an optical axis of the beam. The position of the beam-correcting optical member along a vertical direction is on or adjacent to the position of a supporting point defined in accordance to a structure of the vibration-isolation unit along the vertical direction.

title: LIPID NANOPARTICLES FOR BIOPRODUCTION
assignee: WORCESTER POLYTECH INST [US]; Worcester Polytechnic Institute
inventors: ZIMMER DANIEL [US]; BAILEY-HYTHOLT CHRISTINA M [US]; PERREAULT LUKE R [US] et al.
abstract: A nonviral gene therapy delivers DNA that allows cells to produce proteins such as their own growth factors in culture media. Lipid nanoparticles provide an effective delivery mechanism into a target cell. By encapsulating plasmid DNA encoding the growth factors with inducible promoters into lipid nanoparticles (LNPs), target cells create their own growth factors reducing a need for expensive growth factors from external sources. DNA expression lasts for several days reducing the amount of reagent needed. The plasmid DNA is more stable than mRNA and proteins, therefore reducing logistical challenges.

title: ENHANCED DELIVERY OF DISEASE-TARGETED NANOBODY-SIRNA CONJUGATES
assignee: OHIO STATE INNOVATION FOUNDATION [US]; OHIO STATE INNOVATION FOUNDATION
inventors: KIMMEL BLAISE [US]; CULKINS COURTNEY [US]; KIMMEL, Blaise et al.
abstract: The present disclosure is directed to nanobody-siRNA conjugates and methods of producing and using same. The nanobody-siRNA conjugates include a nanobody that may direct the conjugate to a target cell. The nanobody-siRNA conjugates are delivered into the target cell via receptor-mediated transcytosis, where the siRNA is delivered to the cell cytoplasm after it exits the endosome. To treat diseases and conditions, such as cancers and autoimmune diseases, the siRNA can be efficiently passed through the BBB to modulate an immune response. The small size of nanobodies allows more efficient targeted delivery than typical antibody systems, while conjugation strategies involving click chemistry permit the multiplexed loading of siRNA to nanobodies for increased therapeutic effects.

title: BRAIDED DISTAL OUTER SHEATH FOR STENT DELIVERY SYSTEM
assignee: BOSTON SCIENT SCIMED INC [US]; BOSTON SCIENTIFIC SCIMED, INC.
inventors: TASSONI JR ANTHONY FRANK [US]; CONNAUGHTON ENDA [IE]; BRENNAN YVONNE [IE] et al.
abstract: A stent delivery system includes an inner member with a distal end region including a visual marker, a stent surrounding the distal end region of the inner member including the visual marker, and an outer sheath slidingly surrounding the stent and the inner member. The outer sheath includes a braided reinforcement layer extending from a distal end to a proximal end of the outer sheath, and the outer sheath includes a proximal region in which the braided reinforcement layer has a first braid density, a distal region in which the braid reinforcement layer has a second braid density lower than the first braid density, and a transition region between the proximal region and the distal region. The distal region of the outer sheath is visually translucent or transparent and overlays the stent, and the visual marker and stent are viewable through the distal region of the outer sheath.

title: ENERGY REPORTING AND NOTIFICATION WITH MULTI-ACCESS MEDIA DELIVERY
assignee: SAMSUNG ELECTRONICS CO LTD [KR]; Samsung Electronics Co., Ltd.
inventors: KOLAN PRAKASH REDDY [US]; Kolan, Prakash Reddy
abstract: Methods, apparatuses, and computer readable media for energy reporting and notification with multi-access media delivery. A method for monitoring energy consumption includes receiving, from a user equipment (UE), first energy consumption information associated with an application session for a service in a multi-access network, receiving, from a plurality of network entities in a plurality of access paths between the UE and the multi-access network, second energy consumption information, and determining, based on the first and second energy consumption information, a total energy consumption value for access paths from a list of access paths. The first and second energy consumption information includes a service identifier corresponding to the service, a start time, an end time, and an access energy consumption map indicating the list of access paths, including access end points or network functions used to access the service between the start and end times and measured energy consumption attributed.

title: Équipement d’habitacle bistable.
assignee: STELLANTIS AUTO SAS [FR]; CENTRE NAT RECH SCIENT [FR]; CONSERVATOIRE NAT DES ARTS ET METIERS [FR] et al.
inventors: MEGRET KELIANE [FR]; MONNOYER JOCELYN [FR]; DELALANDE STÉPHANE [FR] et al.
abstract: L’invention concerne un équipement (1) d’habitacle pour un véhicule automobile, comprenant une première partie (2) formant une cavité (3), l’équipement (1) comprenant une deuxième partie (11) conçue pour former un obturateur, la deuxième partie (11) comportant un premier état stable, dans lequel la deuxième partie (11) obture la cavité (3), et un deuxième état stable dans lequel la cavité (3) est au moins en partie ouverte vers l’extérieur de la cavité (3), la deuxième partie (11) étant monobloc et formée d’une feuille de matériau pliée et découpée pour former un diaphragme (12) comportant une pluralité de lamelles (13) déplaçables par pliage pour basculer la deuxième partie (11) entre les premier et deuxième états. title: SYSTEME ET PROCEDE DE VERROUILLAGE ET DE DEVERROUILLAGE D’UNE COLONNE DE DIRECTION REGLABLE D’UN VEHICULE AUTOMOBILE, ET COLONNE DE DIRECTION CORRESPONDANTE
assignee: RENAULT SAS [FR]; RENAULT s.a.s

abstract: Ce système de verrouillage et de déverrouillage d’une colonne de direction réglable d’un véhicule automobile comporte une paire d’éléments de commande (3, 4) manipulables manuellement par un utilisateur, un moteur électrique activable sous l’action d’un appui sur les deux éléments de commande et un organe de blocage déplaçable sous l’action du moteur électrique entre une position de verrouillage de la colonne de direction et une position de déverrouillage de la colonne de direction Figure pour l’abrégé : Fig 3

title: Elément de structure bois-béton et procédé de fabrication
assignee: CRUARD CHARPENTE ET CONSTRUCTION BOIS [FR]; BOSTIK SA [FR]; JOUSSELIN PREFABRICATION [FR] et al.
inventors: FERRIER EMMANUEL [FR]; MICHEL LAURENT [FR]; GUILLERM ROMAIN [FR] et al.
abstract: Elément de structure bois-béton et procédé de fabrication L’invention concerne un procédé de fabrication d’éléments de structure (1) formés d’au moins un module de béton (2) et d’au moins module en bois résiné (3) comprenant au moins une poutre dite de renforcement résinée (6). title: Dispositif anti-condensation d’une canalisation d’un système de pile à combustible
assignee: PLASTIC OMNIUM NEW ENERGIES FRANCE [FR]; Plastic Omnium New Energies France
inventors: SCHAEFFER BENJAMIN [BE]; SCHMID DANIEL [BE]; SCHAEFFER, Benjamin et al.
abstract: L'invention concerne un système de pile à combustible (1) comprenant un dispositif anti-condensation (3) d’une canalisation (4).  Selon l'invention, le système de pile à combustible est tel que le dispositif anti-condensation (3) comprend un dispositif de chauffage (5) constitué d’un liquide caloporteur circulant au sein d’une tubulure (6) enroulée autour de la canalisation (4), l’orifice d’entrée (60) du fluide caloporteur dans la tubulure (6) étant disjoint de l’orifice d’entrée (40) ou de l’orifice de sortie (41) de la canalisation (4).  Figure 1.

title: Télescope de type TMA à deux voies optiques
assignee: SAFRAN REOSC [FR]; SAFRAN REOSC
inventors: RIGUET FRANÇOIS [FR]; RODOLFO JACQUES [FR]; RIGUET, François et al.
abstract: Télescope de type anastigmatique à trois miroirs, comprenant au moins un miroir primaire (1), un miroir secondaire (2) placé en avant du miroir primaire (1) de telle manière que les miroirs primaire et secondaire aient des surfaces optiques (1.1, 2.1) en regard l’une de l’autre, un miroir tertiaire (3) ayant une surface optique (3.1) en regard de la surface optique (2.1) du miroir secondaire (2), et un premier capteur optronique (10) placé en regard du miroir tertiaire (3). Le télescope comprend un deuxième capteur optronique (20) placé derrière le miroir secondaire (2) et la surface optique du miroir secondaire (2.1) comprend un traitement dichroïque (2’) agencé pour séparer un flux lumineux incident réfléchi par le miroir primaire (1) en un premier flux lumineux réfléchi vers le premier capteur optronique (10) via le miroir tertiaire (3) et un deuxième flux lumineux transmis au deuxième capteur optronique (20), le premier flux lumineux et le deuxième flux lumineux appartenant à des bandes spectrales différentes et le miroir secondaire (2) étant transparent à la bande spectrale du deuxième flux lumineux. abstract: La présente invention concerne un dispositif (1) de chargement d’éléments (30) plats globalement parallélépipédiques comportant quatre coins (300), une face supérieure (31) et une face inférieure (32), lesdits éléments (30) étant superposés sous la forme d’une pile (3), le chargement étant effectué par un transfert successif depuis ladite pile (3) vers un poste (2) de traitement, ledit poste (2) de traitement présentant une entrée (20) pour recevoir les éléments (30) successifs, éventuellement maintenus par un lien (90) ou similaire pour former un lot, ledit dispositif (1) comprenant au moins :- un outil (4) de préhension adapté pour soulever et déplacer lesdits éléments (30) plats depuis la pile (3) vers l’entrée (20), ledit outil (4) de préhension comprenant au moins un organe (40) de prise,- une unité (5) de contrôle configurée pour exécuter une routine de chargement de ladite entrée (20) dudit poste (2) de traitement à partir de la pile (3) d’éléments (30) plats, - un bras (6) robotisé conçu et agencé pour déplacer ledit outil (4) de préhension au moins entre la pile (3) et l’entrée (20) du poste (2), ledit outil (4) étant positionné à l’extrémité distale dudit bras (6) robotisé,le dispositif (1) étant caractérisé en ce qu’il comprend :- des moyens (7) de détection optique, lesdits moyens (7) comprenant au moins une caméra (70) permettant de détecter pour chaque élément (30) plat à déplacer par ledit outil (4) de préhension, au moins un repère (8), ledit repère (8) se situant sur une des faces (31, 32) dudit élément (30), et en ce que - l’unité (5) de contrôle est configurée pour déterminer et/ou recevoir des coordonnées dudit au moins un repère (8) détecté et pour transmettre des instructions à des moyens de commande (60) d’un actionneur dudit bras (6) robotisé afin d’ajuster le déplacement dudit outil (4) de préhension en fonction des coordonnées dudit au moins un repère (8).  L’invention concerne également un procédé de chargement et un élément (30) comprenant au moins un repère (8) sur une de ses faces (31,32).   Figure pour l’abrégé : Fig.2

title: MOTEUR ELECTRIQUE A BOBINAGE TOROÏDAL ET A FILTRAGE DE COURANTS DE MODE COMMUN.
assignee: ERNEO [FR]; ERNEO
inventors: JAC JULIEN [FR]; ZIEGLER NICOLAS [FR]; JAC, Julien et al.
abstract: L’invention concerne un moteur électrique ou alternateur comportant un rotor comportant p≥1 paire(s) de pôles magnétiques, un stator (100) conformé pour coopérer au niveau d’un entrefer avec ledit rotor pour produire un effort mécanique et/ou une tension électrique induite, ledit stator comportant une pluralité N de bobines (104, 104φ 1, 104φ 2, 104φ 3), caractérisé en ce que :  - Les bobines sont enroulées de manière toroïdale autour du circuit magnétique du stator,  - Les bobines sont enroulées et alimentées de sorte que les flux magnétiques orthoradiaux des courants de mode commun de chaque bobine s’additionnent entre eux, de manière à obtenir une forte inductance, pour réduire lesdits courants de mode commun,  - La pluralité N de bobines vérifie l’équation suivante : N = M x Q x p x k, avec :  - M : nombre de phases,  - k = 1  - Q = nombre de bobines par paire de pôles et par phase. title: Piste d’aviation équipée d’un tel système d’immobilisation
assignee: DERSAHAKIAN HERANT ANDRE [FR]; DERSAHAKIAN Hérant, André
inventors: DERSAHAKIAN HÉRANT ANDRÉ [FR]; DERSAHAKIAN, Hérant, André
abstract: Ce système, logé dans une fosse d’une piste d’aviation, comprend au moins un organe d’immobilisation (1), lequel comporte une ossature (2) fixe par rapport aux parois de la fosse, un tapis de réception (3) destiné à recevoir un train d’atterrissage de l’aéronef, des moyens (4) d’entraînement de ce tapis par rapport à l’ossature, des moyens (5) de freinage de ce tapis, ainsi que des moyens de commande (8) aptes à activer les moyens d’entraînement en réponse à un premier signal, puis désactiver les moyens d’entraînement tout en activant les moyens de freinage, en réponse à un second signal.  Le système selon l’invention prévoit l’immobilisation d’un aéronef en difficulté sans exercer de contraintes significatives sur cet aéronef, qui reste en parfait état de vol. L’invention permet d’éviter toute opération postérieure, visant à contrôler l’intégrité de la structure de l’aéronef. En outre, l’invention permet de s’affranchir de toute indisponibilité technique de la piste. Enfin l’invention trouve son application à l’immobilisation d’aéronefs durant leur phase d’atterrissage, mais aussi de décollage.   Figure pour l’abrégé : [Fig. 3].

abstract: Le procédé de traitement d’un signal analogique issu d’un pixel utilise une méthode dite de double échantillonnage corrélé comportant deux mesures successives de durées entre l’instant de début d’un signal de rampe et l’instant où le signal de pixel croise la rampe.
