title: EYE MODEL WITH BUBBLE MANAGEMENT
assignee: COLLINS NATHANIEL R [US]; SLABBER NICO J [US]; BALAN VIJAY R [US] et al.

abstract: A simulated eye surgical model with a bubble management system that facilitates training of ophthalmic surgical procedures, such as laser-based glaucoma treatment. The simulated eye could also be used for marketing, startup procedures, go/no-go tests, etc. The eye model has a lower core, a corneal dome positioned above the lower core, and an anterior chamber under the corneal dome defining a floor. A bubble reservoir under the anterior chamber defined by the lower core has a capture tube opening to the anterior chamber. A bubble chamber open to the capture tube rises up above the capture tube lower end to an upper portion sealed off from the anterior chamber. The eye model may be rotated 360° about a horizontal axis to transfer bubbles from the anterior chamber to the bubble chamber. An alternative flow-through eye model provides active flushing of bubbles and particulate.

abstract: [0000]    A laser alignment tool for flange fit-up includes tapered centering nuts that seat in flange bolt holes, a mounting bolt to secure the nuts, and a connector rod extending through the nuts. A yoke bracket at the laser end carries a rotatable laser housing with an integrated laser projection system. A manual actuator controls a clamping head that selectively locks the laser housing orientation. Multiple bubble levels on the nuts, rod, and housing provide comprehensive leveling verification. The tool projects a visible laser line across flange surfaces to provide precise visual alignment reference for positioning flanges relative to corresponding components, eliminating inaccuracies of manual alignment techniques while enabling single-operator use with 360-degree rotational adjustment capability for complex angular alignments in oil and gas applications.

title: INSULATING RADIANT BARRIER FACTORY BONDED TO CELLULOSIC SUBSTRATE

abstract: [0000]    An insulating radiant barrier sheathing product formed by directly bonding or applying an insulating radiant barrier (IRB) material to the interior surface (i.e., facing the attic or other interior space) of a wood, wood-based, manufactured wood, or cellulosic panel or structural or sheathing panel. The IRB panel product may be used as a roof sheathing panel, with one or more outer protective and finishing layers applied to the outer surface, such as, but not limited to, an underlayment and roofing shingles. The IRB material may comprise a mixture of a radiant barrier (RB) paint and a granulated insulating material. The granulated insulating material may comprise cork, EPS, XPS, poly-iso, other similar insulating material, or combinations thereof. Alternatively, the IRB material may comprise a reflecting bubble-wrap layer.

title: MULTISTAGE ABSORPTION AND DESORPTION FOR CO2 SEPARATIONS
assignee: MASSACHUSETTS INST TECHNOLOGY [US]; MASSACHUSETTS INSTITUTE OF TECHNOLOGY
inventors: HATTON TREVOR ALAN [US]; NITZSCHE MICHAEL P [US]; MASSEN-HANE MICHAEL EDWARD LEV [US] et al.
abstract: Electrochemically mediated sorbent-based carbon capture systems have recently gained attention as an alternative to traditional thermally based separations. Realizations of such systems typically require a choice between thermodynamically favorable but practically challenging two-stage systems, which combine activation with capture and deactivation with release, or pragmatic but less energy-efficient stepwise four-stage processes decoupling these steps. In this work, we present a thermodynamic assessment of novel multistage systems, which are shown to combine the practicality of sequential unit operations with the thermodynamic favorability of concerted operations. A generalized thermodynamic assessment shows these systems approach the thermodynamic minimum energy of separation in the limit of many stages for an arbitrary sorbent chemistry. Staged desorption is further shown to decrease dramatically the amount of gas in the cell during deactivation, potentially reducing bubble-driven inefficiencies.

The image processing device (24) has: an amplification unit (24a) that amplifies, at a prescribed amplification factor, a signal generated by light incident on the imaging device; a determination unit (24b) that determines whether or not a value pertaining to the wavelength of the light incident on the imaging device is within a prescribed range; and an adjustment unit (24c) that, if the value pertaining to the wavelength of the light deviates from the prescribed range, adjusts the amplification factor so that the value pertaining to the wavelength of the light falls within the prescribed range.

title: FREEZE DESALINATION METHOD VIA INJECTION OF DRY ICE FIELD
assignee: PRD TECH INC [US]; PRD TECH, INC.
inventors: GOVIND RAKESH [US]; GOVIND, Rakesh
abstract: [0000]    The present invention relates to a freeze desalination method where carbon dioxide, in the form of dry ice, is injected into saltwater. The carbon dioxide sublimes, which cause ice to form around the carbon dioxide bubble. The ice bubble floats to the top of the saltwater, and it can then be skimmed off the top and melted. This water has a low concentration of salt. It then can go through further desalination methods, such as reverse osmosis, to further purify the water. The embodiments described herein are not limiting and this can be accomplished by methods not described fully herein. Particularly, the present invention provides a direct freeze desalination (DFD) method wherein solid carbon dioxide pellets are injected into saline water, forming buoyant ice balls that are separated, washed, and melted. The process uniquely integrates CO<2 >recycling, energy-saving compression, and optional mineral recovery to produce potable water with lower energy consumption compared to conventional methods

title: APPARATUS FOR MANUFACTURING SYNGAS THROUGH NANONIZATION OF PULVERIZED COAL
assignee: LEE TAE YOUL [KR]; 이태열
inventors: LEE TAE YOUL [KR]; 이태열
abstract: Disclosed in the present invention is an apparatus for manufacturing a syngas through nanonization of pulverized coal, the apparatus comprising: a pulverizer that ultrafinely pulverizes pulverized coal supplied from a hopper; a plurality of sprayers that pressure-feed and spray, to a cylindrical header in a vortex chamber, the ultrafinely pulverized coal which is ultrafinely pulverized by the pulverizer; a distributor that is installed in a pipeline between the pulverizer and the sprayers and uniformly supplies the ultrafinely pulverized coal to each sprayer; a vortex fan that generates a vortex of air within the vortex chamber to nanonize the ultrafinely pulverized coal by collision and repeated friction at a contact point of centrifugal force and centripetal force caused by generation of air waves; a vacuum chamber that is vertically separated by the vortex chamber and a partition wall; a plurality of spiral branch pipes having one end connected and fixed to all sides of the header and the other end connected to the inside of the vacuum chamber to induce an explosive expansion of the nanonized pulverized coal supplied into the vacuum chamber; a plasma generator that ionizes and radicalizes the nanonized pulverized coal and gas in the vacuum chamber to convert same into syngas; and a gas collector that collects the syngas in the vacuum chamber and stores same.

abstract: A wearable, skin adhesive blood gas monitoring device detects both oxygen and carbon dioxide for accommodating a potential inaccuracy in an oxygen level resulting from excessive carbon dioxide. Photoluminescent films in conjunction with a compact form-factor circuit provide a wearable and unobtrusive device that adheres directly to the epidermal surface for computation of blood gas levels based on transcutaneous and reflective properties of the blood gases. A wireless medium such as Bluetooth® enables communication to a standalone or portable device such as a smartphone or similar personal device having an application (app) for receiving the computed signals. Conditions such as hypercarbia, which tend to result in higher levels of carbaminohemoglobin, or hemoglobin bound to CO2 instead of oxygen, tend to compromise computed oxygen levels by interpreting the CO2-bound hemoglobin as oxygenated blood.

abstract: A non-transitory, processor readable medium stores instructions that, when executed by a processor, causes the processor to receive (1) an indication of a feed gas flow rate measured between a feed gas source and an internal combustion engine (ICE) and (2) an indication of an oxygen enriched air flow rate measured between an air separation unit (ASU) and the ICE. A measured equivalence ratio is determined based on a feed gas composition, the feed gas flow rate, and the oxygen enriched air flow rate. The instructions further cause the processor to generate at least one of a first signal to cause a change in revolution speed of the ICE, a second signal to cause a first flow control device to modify the feed gas flow rate, or a third signal to cause a second flow control device to modify the oxygen enriched air flow rate.

title: Determining Blood-Oxygen Level (SpO2) Using Broadband Spectra To Reduce Or Eliminate Skin-Tone Bias In The Determined SpO2 Value, And/Or Using Broadband Spectra to Determine Pulse Rate

abstract: An embodiment for determining a blood-oxygen level (e.g., SpO2) of a subject includes exposing a body portion of the subject to more than two wavelengths of electromagnetic energy, receiving a portion of the more than two wavelengths redirected by the body portion, converting the received portion of the redirected more-than-two wavelengths into signals each representing a respective group of at least one of the more than two wavelengths, and determining the blood-oxygen level (e.g., SpO2) of the subject in response to the signals. For example, such an embodiment can determine the blood-oxygen level of the subject with little or no skin-tone bias. That is, such an embodiment can determine the blood-oxygen level of the subject independently of the subject's skin tone, i.e., with little or no skin-tone-induced corruption of the determined blood-oxygen level.

inventors: BROWN CARL [GB]; BROWN, Carl
abstract: An item of legwear (10, 50, 70, 80, 90, 100) comprising: a tubular section (12, 82) to pass around a leg, the tubular section comprising of a first end (30) and a second end (16); and a circumferential divider (18, 57, 58) including one or more circumferentially arranged ribs on an inner surface of the tubular section, located between the first end and the second end; wherein the tubular section comprises a plurality of holes (24, 54a, 54b, 55a, 55b, 71, 81, 91), a first hole (24, 54a, 55a, 71, 81, 91) of the plurality of holes being paired symmetrically about the circumferential divider with a second hole (24, 54b, 55b, 71, 81, 91) of the plurality of holes, such that folding of the legwear about the circumferential divider brings the first hole on one side of the circumferential divider to be brought into alignment with the corresponding second hole on the other side of the circumferential divider such that the first and second holes are aligned one atop the other.

title: BIO-INSPIRED MICRO-NANOSTRUCTURED WOUND DRESSING WITH SELF-CLEANING AND HEALING-PROMOTIVE FEATURES
assignee: UNIV HONG KONG SCIENCE & TECH [CN]; THE HONG KONG UNIVERSITY OF SCIENCE AND TECHNOLOGY
inventors: LAM DAVID [CN]; ZHANG ZHE [CN]; LAM, David et al.
abstract: A wound dressing and a method of fabricating the wound dressing are provided. The wound dressing includes a flexible substrate; a plurality of microstructures disposed on a surface of the flexible substrate; and a plurality of nanostructures disposed on the microstructures. The method of fabrication the wound dressing includes forming a flexible substrate; creating a plurality of microstructures on a surface of the flexible substrate; and growing a plurality of nanostructures on the microstructures. The resulting microstructures and nanostructures are configured to repel water and contaminants for self-cleaning of the wound dressing and stimulate wound healing. The flexible, micro-nano wound dressing has good biocompatibility and bactericidal ability, significantly promoting wound healing performance.

title: METHOD FOR SELECTIVELY GROWING ZINC OXIDE NANOWIRES IN NANO-ARRAY PATTERN AND NANO-ARRAY PATTERN SURFACE IN WHICH ZINC OXIDE NANOWIRES ARE SELECTIVELY GROWN
assignee: UNIV WONKWANG CT IND ACAD COOP [KR]; 원광대학교산학협력단
inventors: PARK HYUN HA [KR]; CHO YOUNG SAM [KR]; KIM HEE KYEONG [KR] et al.
abstract: Disclosed are a method for selectively growing zinc oxide nanowires in a nano-array pattern and a nano-array pattern surface in which zinc oxide nanowires are selectively grown. The method for selectively growing zinc oxide (ZnO) nanowires, according to an embodiment of the present invention, may comprise the steps of: manufacturing a nano-array pattern surface; preparing a seed solution containing zinc (Zn) and a reaction solution for growing nanowires; covering the nano-array pattern surface with the seed solution; spin-coating according to predetermined process conditions; and selectively growing the ZnO nanowires by reacting the reaction solution with the nano-array pattern surface which has been spin-coated according to the predetermined reaction conditions.

title: VARIABLE LIGHTING SYSTEM FOR MICRO-DEFECT DETECTION OPTICAL SYSTEM, AND INSPECTION DEVICE COMPRISING SAME
assignee: YUNAM OPTICS INC [KR]; (주) 유남옵틱스
inventors: KIM YOUNGJU [KR]; LEE KANGBAE [KR]; KIM SEUNGHYEON [KR] et al.
abstract: This variable lighting system for a micro-defect detection optical system may comprise a dark-field illuminator for illuminating a subject to be inspected by emitting light thereto at a predetermined angle with respect to the central axis of the subject to be inspected. The dark-field illuminator may include: a light source unit which includes at least two light sources having different wavelengths and is provided such that respective light beams from the light sources are emitted vertically upward; a reflection unit which is provided such that each of the light beams incident vertically upward is reflected at the predetermined angle toward the subject to be inspected, so as to illuminate the subject to be inspected by emitting each of the light beams thereto at the predetermined angle; and a change unit which is provided such that the position of the reflection unit is changed so as to adjust the predetermined angle.

title: PHOTONIC INTEGRATED CIRCUITS FOR NANO-LITHOGRAPHY AND NANO-IMAGING
assignee: UNIV DELFT TECH [NL]; TECHNISCHE UNIVERSITEIT DELFT
inventors: SHARMA NARESH [NL]; LOPEZ RODRIGUEZ BRUNO [NL]; LI ZIZHENG [NL] et al.
abstract: A photonic integrated circuit (PIC 200), an optical system comprising such PIC and a method of processing a sample using such PIC are presented. The PIC comprising a plurality of scanning waveguides (112) and plasmonic nanostructures (114) in a tip of each of the scanning waveguides. The tip of the scanning waveguide is a part of the scanning waveguide where light exists or enters the PIC when in operation.

title: COMPOSITIONS AND METHODS FOR TARGETED DELIVERY OF COMBINATORIAL THERAPEUTICS
assignee: PENN STATE RES FOUND [US]; THE PENN STATE RESEARCH FOUNDATION
inventors: HAYES DANIEL JAMES [US]; YIENGST TYUS [US]; HELTON ANGELA MEI [US] et al.
abstract: Provided are compositions and methods that include use of micro-RNAs and mimics of the micro-RNAs for treating cancer. Specific ratios of micro-RNAs that have enhanced anti-cancer properties for treating prostate, bladder, and head and neck cancer are provided. The micro-RNAs are conjugated to nanoparticles configured so that exposure to light releases the micro-RNAs from the nanoparticles, thereby allowing the microRNAs to elicit anti-cancer effects.

title: ORGANIC-INORGANIC COMPOSITE MEDICAL MATERIAL AND PREPARATION METHOD THEREFOR
assignee: HEBEI CHEMICAL & PHARMACEUTICAL COLLEGE [CN]; 河北化工医药职业技术学院
inventors: ZHAO ZHICAI [CN]; 赵志才
abstract: The present invention is applied to the technical field of composite medical materials. Disclosed is an organic-inorganic composite medical material, the material consisting of organic components and inorganic components, the organic components comprising collagen, hyaluronic acid and chitosan, and the inorganic components being nano-hydroxyapatite and silica nanoparticles. Among the inorganic components of the organic-inorganic composite medical material, the nano-hydroxyapatite has the characteristics of good biocompatibility and osteoconductivity and thus enables the composite material to integrate with human bones; and the silica nanoparticles which have the characteristics of enhancing the strength and stability of materials improve the stability of the composite material. Among the organic components, proteins which are natively present in the human body and mainly comprise collagen can improve the biological activity and flexibility of the composite material, and promote cell growth and tissue regeneration; the collagen provides an attachment and growth environment for cells; and the hyaluronic acid is used for moisturizing and has an effect of promoting cell growth.

title: NANO-SUPERCAPACITOR AND METHOD OF MANUFACTURING THE SAME
assignee: HYUNDAI MOTOR CO LTD [KR]; KIA CORP [KR]; HYUNDAI MOTOR COMPANY et al.
inventors: KWON DAE-SUNG [KR]; YOO IL-SEON [KR]; KIM HYUN-SOO [KR] et al.
abstract: A nano-supercapacitor includes a substrate on which a plurality of grating bars are disposed, an insulating layer disposed on the substrate, a plurality of first side surface electrodes disposed on the insulating layer on first side surfaces of the plurality of grating bars, a plurality of second side surface electrodes disposed on the insulating layers on second side surfaces of the plurality of grating bars, and a nanomaterial coated on surfaces of the plurality of first side surface electrodes and the plurality of second side surface electrodes. The nano-supercapacitor has an increased electrode surface area using a three-dimensional structure and a nanomaterial, and a uniform nanogap defined in a batch process.

title: COMPOSITIONS AND METHODS FOR PERFORMING SPATIALLY RESOLVED TRANSCRIPTOMICS
assignee: NEW YORK GENOME CENTER INC [US]; UNIV COLUMBIA [US]; NEW YORK GENOME CENTER, INC. et al.
inventors: VICKOVIC SANJA [US]; MCKELLAR DAVID [US]; VICKOVIC, Sanja et al.
abstract: Provided herein are compositions and methods for performing micro isoform ST spatial transcriptomics (miST).

title: INTEGRATED NANO SCALE CAPACITIVE PRESSURE SENSOR AND ANALOG PHYSICAL UNCLONABLE FUNCTION (PUF)
assignee: RAYTHEON CO [US]; Raytheon Company
inventors: SAGHIZADEH PARVIZ [US]; GEORGE AARON [US]; KEHL JASON MICHAEL [US] et al.
abstract: A nano-scale capacitive pressure sensor formed of an array of capacitive pressure cells is disclosed. The capacitive pressure sensor is integrated with an ASIC working as a pressure sensor and a PUF. Each of capacitive pressure cells is a hermetically sealed cavity and includes a first stationary conductive membrane and a second conductive membrane that deflects due to a force (pressure change) applied on the array of capacitive pressure sensor cells. A method of manufacturing the nano-scale capacitive pressure sensor is also disclosed.

title: MICRO-LIGHT EMITTING DIODE CHIP AND FORMING METHOD THEREOF, AND AUTOMOBILE LAMP
assignee: JADE BIRD DISPLAY SHANGHAI LTD [CN]; JADE BIRD DISPLAY (SHANGHAI) LIMITED
inventors: WANG HAO [CN]; FENG FENG [CN]; WANG, Hao et al.
abstract: A Micro-Light Emitting Diode (Micro LED) chip and a forming method thereof, and an automobile lamp are provided. The Micro LED chip includes: a first epitaxial layer having a first side and a second side opposite to each other; a plurality of multi-quantum well layers disposed on the first side and in contact with the first epitaxial layer; a plurality of second epitaxial layers disposed on the first side, and each of the plurality of multi-quantum well layers is disposed between the first epitaxial layer and one corresponding second epitaxial layer; and a plurality of conductive mirror layers disposed on the first side, and each of the plurality of conductive mirror layers is electrically connected to one corresponding second epitaxial layer, and surrounds a non-light-emitting side of one corresponding multi-quantum well layer. Photoelectric conversion efficiency of the Micro LED chip is improved, and optical crosstalk is reduced.

title: RECEIVER UNIT FOR MICROWAVE LINK SITE
assignee: ERICSSON TELEFON AB L M [SE]; TELEFONAKTIEBOLAGET LM ERICSSON (PUBL)
inventors: LOGREN MATS [SE]; MALMSTRÖM MARTIN [SE]; LARSSON AGNE [SE] et al.
abstract: There is provided techniques for processing signals by a receiver unit of a microwave link site. A method comprises processing, by an analog processing circuitry, an analog signal as received over the air by an antenna of the microwave link site. The method comprises converting, by an analog-to-digital converter, the analog signal, as processed by the analog processing circuitry, to a first digital signal. The method comprises sending, by a digital signal interface, the first digital signal to at least one further receiver unit of the microwave link site over a digital connection. The method comprises receiving, by the digital signal interface, a respective second digital signal from each of said at least one further receiver unit over the digital connection. The method comprises processing, by a digital processing unit, the first digital signal and said respective second digital signal.

title: STEERABLE BALLOON OCCLUSION MICROCATHETER
assignee: MARSHALL MEDICAL SUZHOU CO LTD [CN]; 苏州马世医疗科技有限公司
inventors: WANG YING [CN]; ZHAO JIANGQI [CN]; 汪颖 et al.
abstract: A steerable balloon occlusion microcatheter, which relates to the technical field of interventional catheters. The steerable balloon occlusion microcatheter comprises an integral balloon structure (231) and a steerable structure (1). The integral balloon (231) and a catheter outer layer (23) are integrally formed and are not bonded or welded to a catheter body (2), which can increase the strength of the balloon (231) and reduce the risks of using in high-pressure environments. The steerable structure (1) enables bending control of the distal end of the catheter body (2), which can improve the efficiency of the catheter passing through the human body lumen, thereby improving the success rate of surgery and the treatment precision, and reducing the risk of surgery and a waste caused by device failure.

title: MIXTURE OF MICROORGANISMS FOR TREATING WASTEWATER
assignee: SEPULVEDA AVILA DANIELA [CL]; VILLOUTA ROMERO GABRIELA [CL]; SEPÚLVEDA AVILA, Daniela et al.
inventors: SEPÚLVEDA AVILA DANIELA [CL]; VILLOUTA ROMERO GABRIELA [CL]; SEPÚLVEDA AVILA, Daniela et al.
abstract: The present invention relates to a mixture of heterotrophic microorganisms comprising the bacterial strains Pedobacter sp., Pseudomonas sp. (extremaustralis) and Variovorax paradoxus. These microorganisms form a biofilm and are able to improve water quality and reduce odorous emissions and pathogenic agents in wastewater, preferably in liquid industrial waste (LIW). The present invention also discloses a device for storing and/or releasing the biofilm produced by the mixture of microorganisms, the use of the biofilm, the mixture or the device for reducing pollutants and volatile compounds in water treatment plants, lagoons, homogenisation wells, retention ponds, inter alia, of the agricultural sector.

title: UNKNOWN VIBRATION COMPENSATOR FOR VEHICLE SYSTEMS HAVING MICRO ELECTROMECHANICAL SYSTEM SCANNING MICROMIRRORS
assignee: FCA US LLC [US]; FCA US LLC
inventors: TAN QINGYUAN [US]; MU WILLIAM [US]; Tan, Qingyuan et al.
abstract: A vibration compensation technique for a micro electromechanical system (MEMS) micromirror includes establishing or accessing a model for the MEMS micromirror, determining a sample of the deflection angle of a mirror plate of the MEMS micromirror, estimating, using the model and defection angle sample, a vibration of the MEMS micromirror, calculating, using the model and the estimated vibration, a vibration compensation, adding the vibration compensation to an input driving voltage signal for a deflection mechanism of the MEMS micromirror to obtain a compensated input driving voltage signal, where the deflection mechanism is configured to control the deflection angle of the mirror plate, and controlling the deflection mechanism using the compensated input driving voltage signal to mitigate or eliminate the vibration of the MEMS micromirror.

title: PREPARATION METHOD OF C-ZIF-67-DERIVED NANO-POROUS CARBON DOPED PDMS MIXED MATRIX MEMBRANE BASED ON TUBULAR CERAMIC CARRIER AND APPLICATION OF PERVAPORATION TO RECOVER ETHANOL
assignee: JILIN UNIV OF CHEMICAL TECHNOLOGY [CN]; Jilin University of Chemical Technology
inventors: YUAN BO [CN]; LI YUZHUANG [CN]; GOU JIAXUAN [CN] et al.
abstract: Described herein is a preparation method of C-ZIF-67-derived nano-porous carbon doped PDMS mixed matrix membrane based on a tubular ceramic carrier and its application in pervaporation of low-concentration ethanol aqueous solution to recover ethanol by synthesizing ZIF-67 crystals and carbonizing the ZIF-67 crystal to obtain C-ZIF-67 filler particles. According to the present application, the nano-porous carbon particles derived from ZIF-67 are prepared by a high-temperature calcination method, and are fully carbonized at a certain calcination temperature, so that the disappearance of characteristic groups after carbonization can reduce the influence of surface electrical properties, and the particle size is reduced. At the same time, the MOF skeleton structure is still retained after carbonization, the pore size is enlarged, and the carbon material also has strong hydrophobicity.

title: HIGH-RELIABILITY MICRO LIGHT-EMITTING DIODE AND METHOD FOR MANUFACTURING THE SAME
assignee: KOREA INSTITUTE OF ENERGY TECH [KR]; Korea Institute of Energy Technology
inventors: KWAK JOON SEOP [KR]; KIM TAE KYOUNG [KR]; KWAK, Joon Seop et al.
abstract: [0000]    A micro-LED includes: a substrate, a first semiconductor layer laminated on an upper surface of the substrate, a first electrode pad formed on an upper surface of the first semiconductor layer, an active layer laminated on an upper surface of the first semiconductor layer, a second semiconductor layer laminated on an upper surface of the active layer, a second electrode pad formed on the second semiconductor layer, and a stress recovery passivation layer configured to recover stress relaxation of the active layer caused by etching, the stress recovery passivation layer comprising a first passivation layer deposited by particles having a first energy on a portion of the upper surface and sidewalls of a mesa structure, where the mesa structure includes the active layer and the second semiconductor layer laminated together by etching.

title: ULTRASOUND IMAGE PROCESSING DEVICE, ULTRASOUND DIAGNOSTIC APPARATUS, AND ULTRASOUND IMAGE PROCESSING PROGRAM
assignee: FUJIFILM CORP [JP]; FUJIFILM Corporation
inventors: ISHIGURO SUGURU [JP]; ISHIGURO, Suguru
abstract: [0000]    A processor provided in an ultrasound image processing device is configured to: generate ultrasound image data corresponding to each of ultrasound probes based on a reception signal output from each of the ultrasound probes; acquire position-and-posture information of each of the ultrasound probes; and generate display image data showing an ultrasound image obtained by one of the ultrasound probes and a probe indicator indicating an ultrasound transmission range of the other ultrasound probe, based on the ultrasound image data corresponding to each of the ultrasound probes and the position-and-posture information.

title: ULTRASOUND IMAGING METHOD AND ULTRASOUND IMAGING SYSTEM
assignee: SHENZHEN MINDRAY BIOMEDICAL ELECTRONICS CO LTD [CN]; SHENZHEN MINDRAY BIO-MEDICAL ELECTRONICS CO., LTD.
inventors: SHEN YINGYING [CN]; SONG XIAOGUO [CN]; CAI JIANXING [CN] et al.
abstract: An ultrasound imaging method and system are provided. Under a full-real-time scanning mode, multiple frames of ultrasound images are sequentially scanned, upon completion of scanning one frame of ultrasound image, the frame is displayed. Under a semi-real-time scanning mode, a scanning action and a first-type action are alternately performed in the scanning period and the pause period respectively, the scanning action includes scanning one frame of ultrasound image in the scanning period or sequentially scanning multiple frames of ultrasound images, and upon completion of scanning one frame of ultrasound image, the frame is displayed; the first-type action includes pausing the scanning of ultrasound images in the pause period, and automatically switching from the pause period to the scanning period when the duration of the pause period reaches.

title: ULTRASOUND DIAGNOSTIC APPARATUS AND METHOD OF CONTROLLING ULTRASOUND DIAGNOSTIC APPARATUS
assignee: FUJIFILM CORP [JP]; FUJIFILM Corporation
inventors: IGARASHI RIKI [JP]; IGARASHI, Riki
abstract: [0000]    Provided are an ultrasound diagnostic apparatus that can easily and accurately correct a contour of an intracardiac cavity and a method of controlling the ultrasound diagnostic apparatus. An ultrasound diagnostic apparatus includes an intracardiac cavity extraction unit that extracts an intracardiac cavity from an ultrasound image in which a heart of a subject is imaged; a correction possibility estimation unit that analyzes a contour of the extracted intracardiac cavity and estimates a possibility of correction in each portion of the contour; a control point specifying unit that specifies a control point on the contour based on the estimated possibility of correction; an input device for a user to perform an input operation; a correction operation reception unit that receives a correction operation performed by the user via the input device on the specified control point; and a contour correction unit that corrects the contour based on the received correction operation.

title: ULTRASOUND DIAGNOSIS SUPPORT APPARATUS AND ULTRASOUND DIAGNOSIS SUPPORT PROGRAM
assignee: FUJIFILM CORP [JP]; FUJIFILM Corporation
inventors: SATO YUKO [JP]; WAKI KOJI [JP]; SATO, Yuko et al.
abstract: A three-dimensional model forming unit acquires a three-dimensional model of a target tissue in which each position is represented by coordinates in a model coordinate system. A probe position and posture detection unit acquires a position and a posture of an ultrasound probe. A position and posture information conversion unit converts position and posture information indicating the position and the posture of the ultrasound probe into converted position and posture information in the model coordinate system. A scanned region specifying unit specifies a model scanning plane in the model coordinate system based on the converted position and posture information in a case in which the target tissue is scanned with an ultrasound beam, and specifies a region of the three-dimensional model through which the model scanning plane passes as a scanned region. A display controller displays the scanned region and an unscanned region in the three-dimensional model in different modes.

title: QUALITY ASSESSMENT METHOD FOR THREE-DIMENSIONAL BREAST ULTRASOUND IMAGE
assignee: GUANGDONG INNOVATION CENTER OF INTELLIGENT ULTRASOUND IMAGING EQUIPMENT CO LTD [CN]; 广东智能化超声成像技术装备创新中心有限公司
inventors: LI DELAI [CN]; GUO JINGFENG [CN]; MA XINYU [CN] et al.
abstract: The present invention relates to the field of three-dimensional ultrasound image processing, and particularly relates to a quality assessment method for a three-dimensional breast ultrasound image. In the present invention, the following technical solution is used: parsing a three-dimensional breast ultrasound image, so as to obtain several cross-sectional image frames; identifying all artifact regions from the cross-sectional image frames, and marking the sizes thereof; on the basis of the aspect ratios and average pixel values of the artifact regions, classifying the artifact regions; on the basis of frame numbers of cross-sectional images where the artifact regions are located, grouping the artifact regions; and compiling statistics on artifact results, and then outputting a quality assessment result. The beneficial effects of the present invention lie in: the sizes and categories of artifact regions can be obtained, and on the basis of frame numbers corresponding to the artifact regions, the categories, quantities and widths of artifact regions present in cross-sectional image frames can be determined, such that a quality assessment result of a three-dimensional breast ultrasound image can be accurately output on this basis, thereby helping a doctor to quickly and accurately determine the quality of an ultrasound examination image, and thus improving the assessment efficiency.

title: AUTOMATIC NIPPLE POSITIONING METHOD FOR THREE-DIMENSIONAL VOLUMETRIC BREAST ULTRASOUND IMAGING
assignee: GUANGDONG INNOVATION CENTER OF INTELLIGENT ULTRASOUND IMAGING EQUIPMENT CO LTD [CN]; 广东智能化超声成像技术装备创新中心有限公司
inventors: LI DELAI [CN]; GUO JINGFENG [CN]; REN JIANGHAO [CN] et al.
abstract: The present invention relates to the technical field of three-dimensional ultrasound imaging, in particular to an automatic nipple positioning method for three-dimensional volumetric breast ultrasound imaging. The present invention uses the following technical solution: determining a two-dimensional coronal plane image having obvious nipple features from two-dimensional coronal plane images parsed from three-dimensional volumetric breast ultrasound data, identifying a plurality of nipple position coordinates from the two-dimensional coronal plane image, removing outlier data from the nipple position coordinates, and finally taking an average to obtain final nipple position coordinates. The present invention has the following beneficial effects: accurate coordinates of nipple positions can be directly calculated from three-dimensional volumetric breast ultrasound data without requiring operators to determine nipple positions on the basis of experiences, thereby effectively improving the efficiency and accuracy of positioning nipple positions, and improving the efficiency of breast examination.

title: ULTRASOUND NEEDLE NAVIGATION SYSTEMS WITH ADAPTIVE DERIVATION FOR TWO UNCERTAIN TRANSFORMATION MATRICES
assignee: UNIV NAT TAIWAN HOSPITAL [TW]; National Taiwan University Hospital
inventors: HSIEH CHENG-YUAN [TW]; WANG YU-SHUN [TW]; YANG HAN-PO [TW] et al.
abstract: The provided ultrasound needle navigation system includes a tracking station, a transducer tracker disposed on the tail of an ultrasound transducer, a needle tracker disposed on the tail of a needle, a needle image detection module, and a processing unit. The needle image detection module acquires a dynamic sequence of measured needle shaft coordinate system from an ultrasound image sequence. The processing unit generates multiple estimated transducer tracker to scanning plane transformation matrices and multiple estimated needle tracker to needle tip transformation matrices, thereby generating multiple sequences of estimated needle shaft coordinate systems. To minimize the estimation losses between the measured and estimated needle shaft coordinate systems, the system iteratively updates the estimated transformation matrices to obtain a final transducer tracker to scanning plane transformation matrix and a final needle tracker to needle tip transformation matrix, thereby enabling three-dimensional needle navigation across different ultrasound transducers and needles.

title: ENHANCED VISIBILITY OF ABLATION DEVICES USING DOPPLER ULTRASOUND
assignee: NEUWAVE MEDICAL INC [US]; Neuwave Medical, Inc.
inventors: DUFF JONATHAN [US]; BRUNKOW JASON [US]; DUFF, Jonathan et al.
abstract: [0000]    A system is disclosed comprising an ablation probe and a controller. The ablation probe comprises a shaft including a stick region, an antenna extending from the shaft, and a cooling tube extending within the shaft. The controller is operable to convey first and second flow rates of coolant through the cooling tube. The first flow rate is sufficient to cause the ablation probe to vibrate, but insufficient to cause tissue to couple to the stick region. The second flow rate is sufficient to cause tissue to couple to the stick region. While conveying the first flow rate of coolant through the cooling tube, the controller is operable to emit, from an ultrasound transducer, a sound wave toward the ablation probe, receive, at the ultrasound transducer, a reflected sound wave from the ablation probe, and display, on a display, an image corresponding to the reflected sound wave.

title: SYSTEM AND METHOD FOR CHARACTERIZING AN ELECTRICAL DISCHARGE DRILLING PROCESS USING ULTRASOUND
assignee: PRATT & WHITNEY CANADA [CA]; PRATT & WHITNEY CANADA CORP.
inventors: ING VISAL [CA]; LETENDRE ETIENNE [CA]; MANSUR RODRIGUES FILHO JORGE FRANKLIN [CA] et al.
abstract: [0000]    Systems and methods for characterizing an electrical discharge drilling process using ultrasound are provided. The characterization of the electrical discharge drilling process facilitates estimating cycle times and electrode consumptions for future electrical discharge drilling tasks. An electrical discharge drilling machine includes a base for supporting a workpiece, an electrode operable to drill a hole in the workpiece using an electrical discharge, and an ultrasonic probe installed on the base and oriented toward the workpiece when the workpiece is supported by the base.

title: SYSTEM AND METHOD FOR SYNTHESIZING FETAL HEARTBEAT AUDIO SIGNATURE
assignee: KONINKLIJKE PHILIPS NV [NL]; KONINKLIJKE PHILIPS N.V.
inventors: BHARAT SHYAM [NL]; HERBST ELIZABETH [NL]; FLANNERY SEAN [NL] et al.
abstract: A system and method for synthesizing a fetal heartbeat. A sequence of ultrasound images of a fetus, acquired in a non-Doppler imaging mode and capturing a heart of the fetus, is received and processed to generate an audio signal representative of the heartbeat of the fetus.

title: SURFACE DISCONTINUITIES IN THE FORM OF ECHOGENIC MARKERS TO IMPROVE ECHOGENICITY ON ROUND MANDRELS
assignee: BOSTON SCIENT SCIMED INC [US]; Boston Scientific Scimed, Inc.
inventors: BARNETT STEPHANE GAREAU [CA]; MITCHELL OWEN [CA]; ZAKAIB SCOTT [CA] et al.
abstract: [0000]    A transseptal guidewire with enhanced echogenicity and markers to facilitate precise positioning during transseptal puncture procedures. The guidewire comprises a flexible elongate body with a distal portion having echogenic markers formed by mechanical deformation of the guidewire surface, creating surface discontinuities that reflect ultrasound waves. These echogenic markers improve visibility under ultrasound imaging, allowing for accurate positioning of the guidewire tip at the target location. The guidewire also includes markers at the proximal end that provide visual or tactile feedback on the relative position of the guidewire with respect to a supporting member, such as a sheath or dilator. The proximal markers enable intuitive positioning and safe advancement of the guidewire during the procedure. By enhancing guidewire visibility and providing positioning feedback, improves the safety and efficiency of transseptal puncture procedures while maintaining compatibility with standard techniques and equipment.

title: COMPOSITE MATCHING LAYER
assignee: KONINKLIJKE PHILIPS NV [NL]; KONINKLIJKE PHILIPS N.V.
inventors: SONG JUNHO [NL]; SUDOL WOJTEK [NL]; SONG, Junho et al.
abstract: Composite matching layer and method of fabricating composite matching layers for ultrasound transducers. A method of fabricating composite matching layers comprises mechanically shaping a high-impedance material (120) to yield a trapezoidal-shaped bar or a plurality of parallel pyramidal shaped columns, and fill grooves between said trapezoidal-shaped bars or plurality of parallel pyramidal shaped columns with a low-impedance material (130).

title: STENT DETECTION AND GROUPING FOR POST PERCUTANEOUS INTERVENTION INTRAVASCULAR IMAGING
assignee: BOSTON SCIENT SCIMED INC [US]; BOSTON SCIENTIFIC SCIMED, INC.
inventors: NARVESON VIOLA KIM HOLMAN [US]; O'BRIEN GRETA [US]; THIPPESWAMY ACHALA [US] et al.
abstract: The present disclosure provides to process intravascular images, such as intravascular ultrasound (IVUS) image frames, to detect stents represented in the images and to group detected stents into stent segments. The grouped stent segments can be visually represented as part of a graphical user interface (GUI) and a user can navigate through the stent segments via the GUI to review stent assessment information on a per stent segment basis.

title: TREATMENT SUPPORT APPARATUS AND TREATMENT SUPPORT PROGRAM
assignee: FUJIFILM CORP [JP]; FUJIFILM Corporation
inventors: ASAFUSA KATSUNORI [JP]; DOKKO SHUICHI [JP]; ISHIDA KOICHI [JP] et al.
abstract:
[0001]    It is possible to easily specify a region of a treatment target portion in ultrasound volume data.
[0002]    A three-dimensional model formation unit forms a three-dimensional model of a target tissue. The three-dimensional model has information indicating a region occupied by a treatment target portion in the target tissue. A volume data formation unit forms ultrasound volume data based on reception signals obtained by transmitting and receiving ultrasound to and from the target tissue with an ultrasound probe 18a. The ultrasound volume data is defined in an ultrasound data coordinate system based on a position and an orientation of the ultrasound probe. A coordinate system conversion unit defines the three-dimensional model in the ultrasound data coordinate system by performing alignment between the three-dimensional model and the ultrasound volume data. A treatment target portion specifying unit specifies a region of a treatment target portion in the ultrasound volume data based on the treatment target portion included in the three-dimensional model defined in the robot coordinate system.

title: CATHETER DISTAL TIP FOR PERFORMING SENSOR ASSISTED NAVIGATION, CATHETER ASSEMBLY, AND ROBOTICALLY ASSISTED INTERVENTIONAL DEVICES
assignee: CREGANNA UNLIMITED COMPANY [IE]; TYCO ELECTRONICS LTD UK [GB]; Creganna Unlimited Company et al.
inventors: HART STEPHEN [US]; MCDERMOTT BERNARD [IE]; REGUS CRISTIAN S [US] et al.
abstract: [0000]    A steerable catheter distal tip for performing sensor assisted navigation for a catheter assembly and a robotically assisted interventional device. The steerable catheter distal tip for performing sensor assisted navigation includes an ultrasonic transducer system arranged to surround a circumference of the catheter distal tip, wherein the ultrasonic transducer system is operable to generate distance signals indicative of distances between a plurality of sensing locations on the circumference of the catheter distal tip from walls of a cavity in which the catheter distal tip is located in operation; and a signal processing unit comprising a controller, which is operable to cause the catheter distal tip being steered along a defined navigation path based on the distance signals.

title: SYSTEM AND METHOD FOR PERFORMING KEYWORD-ASSISTED SEMANTIC SEARCHING
assignee: INTUIT INC [US]; INTUIT INC.
inventors: RASTOGI PANKAJ [US]; AWASTHI SPRIHA [US]; KUMAR DEEPAK [US] et al.
abstract: [0000]    A system and method are provided for performing keyword-assisted semantic searching.

title: METHODS AND APPARATUS FOR ASSISTED FREQUENCY OFFSET ESTIMATION IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATIONS
assignee: MEDIATEK INC [CN]; MEDIATEK INC.
inventors: TSAI CHIOU-WEI [CN]; WU WEI-DE [CN]; TSAI TAI-CHENG [CN] et al.
abstract: Methods and apparatus pertaining to assisted frequency offset estimation in wireless communications are described. An apparatus may transmit a first uplink signal or channel to a network node. The apparatus may receive a first downlink signal or channel carrying information regarding a frequency offset from the network node. The frequency offset may be determined based on the first uplink signal or channel. The apparatus may apply or determine whether to apply the frequency offset to at least one of a second downlink signal or channel and a second uplink signal or channel.

title: SYSTEM AND METHOD FOR VACUUM-ASSISTED MECHANICAL BOARD TO BOARD LATCHING INTERFACE SYSTEM FOR ATE TEST CELLS
assignee: INTEST EMS LLC [US]; INTEST EMS, LLC
inventors: FONG JASON [US]; CARRASCO BALTAZAR S [US]; FONG, Jason et al.
abstract: [0000]    A hybrid vacuum-assisted mechanical latch system for a semiconductor automatic test equipment (ATE) system. The system applies a temporary vacuum from a test interface assembly (TIA), having spring-loaded electrical contacts, to a probe interface board assembly (PIBA) on a first TIA side and to a probe card assembly (PCA) on a second TIA side after the PIBA and PCA are placed into contact with the TIA. A displaceable latch plate having plural projections on a first edge and plural projections on a second edge is included on the TIA. The PIBA side in contact with the TIA has corresponding plural receptacles as does the PCA side in contact with the TIA. When vacuum is applied, the plural projections seat themselves within the plural receptacles and then the latch plate is displaced, thereby locking the PIBA and PCA in a stable electrical contact where spring forces are 700 lbf-2200 lbf.

title: Dynamic Compatibility-Based Device Deployment
assignee: ZEBRA TECH CORP [US]; Zebra Technology Corporation
inventors: SOOD AJAY [IN]; THOMAS SONIA B [IN]; REDDY C R GREESHMA [IN] et al.
abstract: A method includes: receiving a deployment request associated with a device; selecting a profile identifier from a plurality of profile identifiers based on the deployment request, each of the profile identifiers associated with respective device-assisted tasks; retrieving, based on the selected profile identifier, a reference attribute corresponding to the device-assisted tasks associated with the selected profile identifier; obtaining a current attribute corresponding to the device; comparing the reference attribute with the current attribute; and presenting, based on the comparison, an indicator of compatibility between the device and the device-assisted tasks associated with the selected profile identifier.

assignee: RTX CORP [US]; RTX Corporation

title: HIGHER ORDER MODE BULK ACOUSTIC WAVE DEVICE WITH FRAME STRUCTURE
assignee: SKYWORKS GLOBAL PTE LTD [SG]; Skyworks Global Pte. Ltd.
inventors: SHIN KWANG JAE [KR]; BADER STEFAN [US]; CAO ZONGLIANG [US] et al.
abstract: [0000]    Aspects of this disclosure relate to a bulk acoustic wave having a higher order mode as a main mode. The bulk acoustic wave device includes a frame structure in a frame region and a piezoelectric layer that has an engineered region in at least part of the frame region. Related acoustic wave filters, multiplexers, radio frequency modules, radio frequency systems, wireless communication devices, methods of manufacture, and methods of filtering are disclosed.

title: MULTI-TUBULAR CEMENT BOND EVALUATION WITH ELECTROMAGNETIC AND ACOUSTIC JOINT PROCESSING
assignee: HALLIBURTON ENERGY SERVICES INC [US]; Halliburton Energy Services, Inc.
inventors: WU XIANG [US]; JONES CHRISTOPHER MICHAEL [US]; FOUDA AHMED [US] et al.
abstract: [0000]    A method and system for evaluating a cement bond. The method may include disposing a logging tool in a wellbore. The logging tool may include an electromagnetic (EM) sub and an acoustic sub. The method may further comprise transmitting an EM field from the EM sub into one or more tubulars to energize the one or more tubulars with the EM field and measuring a secondary EM field in the one or more tubulars with a EM receive. The method may further comprise transmitting a shaped acoustic signal from the acoustic sub into one or more tubulars and formation, measuring a result signal with the acoustic sub to form one or more acoustic measurements, estimating one or more pipe parameters from the eddy current, and evaluating a cement bond based at least in part on the one or more pipe parameters and the one or more acoustic measurements.

assignee: RTX CORP [US]; RTX Corporation
inventors: YAZICI MURAT [US]; BREAULT ANDREW E [US]; MORTON JEFFREY T [US] et al.
abstract: [0000]    A fan exit guide vane with acoustic treatment including a leading edge and a trailing edge opposite chordwise from the leading edge; a radially inner attachment region opposite spanwise from a radially outer attachment region; a span dimension extending between the radially inner attachment region and the radially outer attachment region; a chord dimension extending between the leading edge and the trailing edge; a pressure side opposite a suction side of the fan exit guide vane; and an acoustic zone integrally formed within the fan exit guide vane.

title: ARRANGEMENT DESIGN SUPPORT METHOD FOR ACOUSTIC DEVICE AND INFORMATION PROCESSING DEVICE
assignee: YAMAHA CORP [JP]; YAMAHA CORPORATION
inventors: MIWA AKIHIRO [JP]; TANAKA KENICHI [JP]; HANADA KOSUKE [JP] et al.
abstract: [0000]    An arrangement design support method for microphone includes receiving an input condition including an acoustic space and positions of a plurality of objects in the acoustic space, calculating an arrangement distribution of the microphone corresponding to the received input condition in the received acoustic space, outputting the calculated arrangement distribution, and correcting a position of another object in conjunction with correction of a position of at least one object among the plurality of objects based on information on an installation condition included in each of the plurality of objects, in a state where a request for correction of the input condition is received.

title: UNIFIED MICROPHONE AND LOUDSPEAKER TO REDUCE ACOUSTIC COUPLING
assignee: BIAMP SYS LLC [US]; Biamp Systems, LLC
inventors: LUTZO AARON ANTHONY [US]; BEHURA SAMARTH [US]; HUGHES II CHARLES EMORY [US] et al.
abstract: [0000]    The example embodiments are directed to a unified loudspeaker and microphone apparatus which reduce acoustic coupling between the loudspeaker and microphone. In one example, the apparatus may include a housing, a loudspeaker integrated into the housing, a microphone integrated into the housing, and an air gap disposed in between the loudspeaker and the microphone. The loudspeaker, the microphone, and the air gap may be arranged along a common plane within the housing.

title: SILICON ENHANCED BULK ACOUSTIC RESONATOR
assignee: SITIME CORP [US]; SITIME CORPORATION
inventors: VIGEVANI GABRIELE [US]; KUYPERS JAN HENRIK [US]; HAGELIN PAUL MERRITT [US] et al.
abstract: A frequency-controlled device includes a silicon layer, a piezoelectric layer coupled to the silicon layer, and top and bottom electrically conductive layers coupled to the piezoelectric layer. In certain embodiments, an oxide layer is disposed between the silicon and piezoelectric layers. The device operates as a bulk acoustic resonator, providing high frequency stability and a high quality factor across a wide temperature range. The resonant structure may incorporate doped silicon and silicon dioxide layers to optimize temperature compensation and phase noise performance. This enables precision timing applications in the 500 MHz to 3 GHz frequency range. The design reduces sensitivity to circuit variations and enhances long-term frequency stability, making the device suitable for use in oscillators and other frequency control applications.

title: SYSTEMS AND METHODS FOR LAYOUT ESTIMATION OF ACOUSTIC DEVICES IN SIMULTANEOUS PLAYBACK CONDITIONS
assignee: HARMAN INT IND [US]; HARMAN INTERNATIONAL INDUSTRIES, INCORPORATED
inventors: KOVALYOV ANTON [US]; GANGULY ANSHUMAN [US]; BASTYR KEVIN J [US] et al.
abstract: Systems and methods are herein provided for imperceptible layout estimation of a plurality of acoustic devices. In one example, a method comprises: watermarking each host audio signal from each of the plurality of acoustic devices with a different watermark; simultaneously transmitting the differently watermarked audio signals from each loudspeaker of each of the plurality of acoustic devices; recording the watermarked audio played by the plurality of loudspeakers using one or more microphones at each device; determining time-of-arrivals (TOAs) and TOA confidence levels for each loudspeaker-microphone pair in the system based on the recorded microphone signals; and estimating a layout of the plurality of devices based on the TOAs and confidence levels in a robust manner to outlier measurements.

title: ACOUSTIC STRUCTURE WITH ARRAY OF INTERCONNECTED RESONATORS, AND ANTI-ICING SYSTEM
assignee: RTX CORP [US]; RTX Corporation
inventors: WINKLER JULIAN [US]; REIMANN CRAIG A [US]; HOMMA KENJI [US] et al.
abstract: [0000]    A gas turbine engine includes a fan delivering air into a bypass duct defined between a nacelle and an inner core housing. The inner core housing receives a compressor section, a turbine section and a combustor. The nacelle has an inner periphery with a forwardmost point, and receives an acoustic structure on the inner periphery adjacent the forwardmost point. The acoustic structure is defined by a three dimensional array of interconnected resonators, with the interconnected resonators extending in a radial direction, a circumferential direction and an axial direction all defined about a rotational axis of the engine, with the interconnected resonators having a larger cross-sectional area central body, and six members connecting the central body of the resonators to adjacent resonators at respective central bodies. A perforated face sheet is inward of the three dimensional array of interconnected resonator and an anti-icing system.

title: ACOUSTIC WAVE DEVICE, FILTER, AND MULTIPLEXER
assignee: TAIYO YUDEN KK [JP]; TAIYO YUDEN CO., LTD.

abstract: An acoustic wave device includes a piezoelectric layer, and a pair of interdigital electrodes provided on the piezoelectric layer, wherein each of the interdigital electrodes includes electrode fingers, dummy electrode fingers and a bus bar having a side surface to which the electrode fingers and the dummy electrode fingers are connected, the pair of interdigital electrodes includes gap regions and an intersection region, the gap regions are arranged along an arrangement direction of the electrode fingers, the intersection region includes an edge region and a central region, and a weight per unit length in the longitudinal direction of a single-layer or multilayer film including a metal film of the electrode fingers provided on the piezoelectric layer at a position where each of the electrode fingers is provided is larger in the edge region than in the central region.

title: SOUND ABSORPTION BODY AND METHOD FOR ITS PRODUCTION
assignee: UNIV PISA [IT]; UNIVERSITÀ DI PISA
inventors: LECCESE FRANCESCO [IT]; SALVADORI GIACOMO [IT]; CROCE PAOLO [IT] et al.
abstract: A sound-absorption body (100) for attenuating frequencies below 500Hz, having a sound-absorption surface (101) configured to be exposed in an environment (200) where acoustic emissions (50) to be attenuated are present. A plurality of through-openings (102) is formed in the sound-absorption surface (101 ), and a corresponding plurality of acoustic attenuation elements (110) are provided, each defining a blind acoustic attenuation path. The acoustic attenuation elements (110) extend from an opposite side of the sound-absorption surface (101 ) each starting from a through opening (102). Each blind acoustic attenuation element (110) extends along an axis (111) having a predetermined length, has a main diameter (112), comprises a plurality of annular partitions (113) having an internal hole (114) with a restricted diameter (115) less than or equal to 95% of the main diameter (112) and terminates with a closed end (116). The invention provides a sound-absorption body with low thickness, high absorption of low-frequency acoustic emissions and ease of manufacture. The restricted diameter (115) can be adjustable, and the axis (111) of the blind acoustic attenuation path can include an initial curve, so that the acoustic attenuation elements (110) are parallel to the sound-absorption surface (101 ).

title: COMPACT SPEAKER
assignee: SONOS INC [US]; Sonos, Inc.
inventors: SCHEEK TIMOTHY RUBEN [NL]; HUNTER-JONES BRIDGET [US]; BIRKENBACK VICTORIA LYNNE [US] et al.
abstract: [0000]    An example speaker assembly includes a housing having a front face, a rear face, and a side wall, the housing including one or more acoustic ports formed in the side wall, and a transducer at least partially disposed within the housing. The transducer includes first and second membranes arranged coaxially about a transducer axis in an opposed relation to each other and configured to move towards and away from the front face along the transducer axis, wherein the transducer axis is parallel to the side wall, and a driver assembly configured to drive the first and second membranes to produce acoustic energy. The speaker assembly is configured to direct first acoustic energy from the first membrane to an exterior of the housing via the front face, and to direct second acoustic energy from the second membrane to the exterior of the housing via the one or more acoustic ports.

title: INFORMATION PROCESSING METHOD, INFORMATION PROCESSING SYSTEM, AND PROGRAM
assignee: YAMAHA CORP [JP]; ヤマハ株式会社
inventors: SUZUKI MASAHIRO [JP]; 鈴木　正博
abstract: An information processing system 100 comprises: an input data acquisition unit 21 that acquires input data A including a condition token X designating any of a plurality of different processes and an acoustic token Y representing a frequency characteristic of an acoustic signal S; and an output data generation unit 22 that, by processing the input data A by a machine-trained generation model M, generates output data B representing a result of executing a process designated by the condition token X among the plurality of processes on the acoustic signal S represented by the acoustic token Y.

title: Method for Synthesizing Nitrogen-Containing Metabolites Using Yeast
assignee: META ORGANIC TECH INC [KR]; Meta Organic Technology Inc
inventors: AHN YI YOUNG [KR]; HAN KYUNG HEE [KR]; YOUN DONG GI [KR] et al.

title: ULTRASONIC DIAGNOSTIC APPARATUS
assignee: CANON MEDICAL SYSTEMS CORP [JP]; CANON MEDICAL SYSTEMS CORPORATION
inventors: SATO HARUKA [JP]; NISHIHARA KURAMITSU [JP]; KAKEE AKIHIRO [JP] et al.
abstract: [0000]    An ultrasonic diagnostic apparatus according to an embodiment includes a sample gate setting unit that sets a sample gate including a region where blood flow information of a subject is acquired on an ultrasonic image of the subject, an acquisition unit that acquires a plurality of blood vessel width candidates based on at least one of reception information of ultrasonic waves of the subject and examination information for a blood vessel at a position where the sample gate is set, and an adjustment unit that adjusts the region included in the sample gate based on the plurality of blood vessel width candidates.

title: YEAST CAPABLE OF HIGH-LEVEL PRODUCTION OF L-METHIONINE
assignee: MUSASHI SEIMITSU KOGYO KK [JP]; NATIONAL UNIV CORPORATION NARA INSTITUTE OF SCIENCE AND TECHNOLOGY [JP]; 武蔵精密工業株式会社 et al.
inventors: ISOGAI SHOTA [JP]; NISHIMURA AKIRA [JP]; INOUE AKIKO [JP] et al.
abstract: [Problem] To provide, for example, a mutant Met13 protein suitable for high-level production of methionine. [Solution] The problem is solved by a mutant methylenetetrahydrofolate reductase protein (Met13 protein) having a Ser443Phe substitution, the mutant Met13 protein comprising any one of the following amino acid sequences (a) to (c): (a) an amino acid sequence that is set forth in SEQ ID NO: 1 or that has a Ser443Phe substitution in an amino acid sequence encoded by a MET13 gene of a yeast belonging to the genus Saccharomyces; (b) an amino acid sequence that is derived from the amino acid sequence of (a) by deletion, substitution, or addition of one or several amino acids and has the function of a Met13 protein; and (c) an amino acid sequence that has 90% or more sequence identity with the amino acid sequence of (a) and has the function of a Met13 protein.

title: METHOD AND DEVICE FOR PREDICTING THREE-DIMENSIONAL STRESS-STRAIN CURVE BY USING ULTRASONIC WAVE
assignee: FOUNDATION SOONGSIL UNIV INDUSTRY COOPERATION [KR]; 숭실대학교산학협력단
inventors: PARK SEONG HYUN [KR]; 박성현
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a method and device for predicting a three-dimensional stress-strain curve by using an ultrasonic wave. More specifically, the method comprises the steps of: emitting an ultrasonic signal to a local area of a specimen with an ultrasonic transceiver to measure an ultrasonic signal; inputting the measured ultrasonic signal to an artificial intelligence model that has been trained to calculate and output a first parameter on the basis of an input ultrasonic signal; inputting the first parameter to a Ramberg-Osgood model when the first parameter calculated on the basis of the measured ultrasonic signal is output from the artificial intelligence model; scanning the ultrasonic transceiver to repeatedly perform each of the above steps for the next local area of the specimen when a second parameter calculated on the basis of the first parameter is output from the Ramberg-Osgood model; and visualizing a three-dimensional stress-strain curve for the entire area of the specimen when all the second parameters for the entire area of the specimen are collected.

title: MULTI-CHANNEL VIBRATOR AND ULTRASONIC DEVICE INCLUDING THE SAME
assignee: LG ELECTRONICS INC [KR]; LG ELECTRONICS INC.
inventors: HONG SUNGHO [KR]; SONG HOSEONG [KR]; LIM GUEISAM [KR] et al.
abstract: [0000]    A multi-channel vibrator for generating ultrasonic waves, can include a plurality of electrode channels located on one surface of the multi-channel vibrator, each electrode channel being spaced apart from each other. The plurality of electrode channels have different diameters from each other, and have one same center portion. Each of the plurality of electrode channels includes a current connection part connecting to one end of a wire for inputting an electric signal to a corresponding one of the electrode channels. Further, at least one of the plurality of electrode channels includes an extension having a portion extending to an outer side of the multi-channel vibrator and having the current connection part located on the extension.

title: METHOD AND COLLIDER SYSTEM FOR PRODUCTION OF GRAPHENE
assignee: LICITAR ANTONIJO [HR]; SIMA MARC [ES]; LICITAR, Antonijo et al.
inventors: LICITAR ANTONIJO [HR]; SIMA MARC [ES]; LICITAR, Antonijo et al.
abstract: The present disclosure describes systems and methods for producing graphene. A method for producing graphene may include dispersing a graphite starting material in an aqueous solution to form a graphite solution; performing a collision treatment on the graphite solution to form a graphene foam; and separating the graphene foam to isolate graphene. A system for producing graphene may include an inlet for introducing a solution; a collider including a core for accelerating the solution; an ultrasonic mixer operatively connected to the collider such that the solution is passed from the collider to the ultrasonic mixer; and a foam separator operatively connected to the ultrasonic mixer such that the solution is passed from the ultrasonic mixer to the foam separator.

title: RESPIRATORY TREATMENT SUPPORT SYSTEM, SERVER, AND DISPLAY DEVICE
assignee: FUKUDA DENSHI KK [JP]; FUKUDA DENSHI CO., LTD.
inventors: YOSHIOKA ERIKO [JP]; TAKAHASHI EIJI [JP]; Yoshioka, Eriko et al.
abstract: [0000]    A respiratory treatment support system according to the present disclosure includes: a respiratory treatment device; a server communicably connected to the respiratory treatment device; and an information terminal communicably connected to the server, in which the server receives operational information on the respiratory treatment device from the respiratory treatment device, stores the operational information and calculates a statistical value and a histogram of the operational information, and is capable of transmitting the statistical value and the histogram to the information terminal.

title: FUEL TREATMENT SYSTEM AND SHIP INCLUDING SAME
assignee: HD KOREA SHIPBUILDING & OFFSHORE ENG CO LTD [KR]; 에이치디한국조선해양 주식회사
inventors: AHN YEONG JONG [KR]; KIM DONG YOUNG [KR]; SONG JI HYEON [KR] et al.
abstract: A fuel treatment system according to an embodiment of the present invention comprises: a fuel flow unit that supplies fuel to a consumption site; a fuel collection unit that uses a cleaning agent to treat fuel discharged from the consumption site or the fuel flow unit; and a wastewater treatment unit for treating wastewater stored in the fuel collection unit, wherein the wastewater treatment unit comprises a membrane treatment section provided with a membrane through which the fuel contained in the wastewater selectively passes.

title: CLOTHING TREATMENT APPARATUS
assignee: LG ELECTRONICS INC [KR]; 엘지전자 주식회사
inventors: KWON SUNKU [KR]; OH JEONGEON [KR]; 권선구 et al.
abstract: Provided in the present invention is a clothing treatment apparatus that performs any course of treating clothes. According to one embodiment, a clothing treatment apparatus comprises: a cabinet; an inner case disposed inside the cabinet and providing an accommodating space in which clothes are placed; a machine compartment disposed below the inner case and generating at least one of hot air and steam supplied into the accommodating space; and a moving hanger configured to shake the placed clothes at a set speed, wherein the moving hanger includes: a plurality of hanger units that place the clothes inside the accommodating space; and a driving unit disposed outside the accommodating space and providing power for shaking the hanger units, wherein the set speed is changed (i) at a peak section of an envelope of a phase current applied to or outputted from the driving unit or (ii) at a point in time when a moving direction of the hanger units is changed.

title: LAUNDRY TREATMENT APPARATUS
assignee: XIAOMI TECHNOLOGY WUHAN CO LTD [CN]; XIAOMI SMART APPLIANCES WUHAN CO LTD [CN]; BEIJING XIAOMI MOBILE SOFTWARE CO LTD [CN] et al.
inventors: CHEN YINGHUA [CN]; ZHANG QI [CN]; YAN LONG [CN] et al.
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a laundry treatment apparatus, which includes a cabinet, and a first cylinder and a second cylinder disposed within the cabinet. The second cylinder is located at a side upper part of the first cylinder, and a vibration damping structure for the second cylinder is disposed below the second cylinder. With the above technical solution, a limited space below the second cylinder can be fully utilized while meeting the vibration damping requirement of the second cylinder.

title: Plating Device for Reduced-Pressure Plating Treatment And Reduced-Pressure Plating Treatment Method
assignee: MITOMO SEMICON ENG CO LTD [JP]; MITOMO SEMICON ENGINEERING CO., LTD.; MITOMO SEMICON ENGINEERING CO., LTD
inventors: UCHIUMI YUJI [JP]; UCHIUMI, YUJI
abstract: [0000]    A plating treatment technique with which influence of air bubbles or the like can be suppressed as much as possible in plating treatment on micro vias or trenches, and a plurality of plating treatments can be handled by one plating equipment. Plating equipment for vacuum plating treatment including: a plating tank including an opening portion having a seal that prevents leakage of a plating solution, and a solution supply section and a solution discharge section for the plating solution; a rotating unit for the plating tank; an in-tank decompression unit that reduces a pressure in an in-tank space; and an object-to-be-plated back surface press cover including a back surface decompression unit that presses a peripheral edge of an object to be plated placed at the opening portion to decompress in a back space formed on a back surface side of the object to be plated.

title: TREATMENT OF CANCER WITH A KRAS INHIBITOR
assignee: ALTEROME THERAPEUTICS INC [US]; ALTEROME THERAPEUTICS, INC.
inventors: MURPHY ERIC ANTHONY [US]; ARANDA RUTH [US]; CHI ANDREW [US] et al.
abstract: Provided herein are compositions and methods for the treatment of cancer. Said compositions comprise a KRAS inhibitor.

title: HANDPIECE FOR SKIN TREATMENT
assignee: EUNSUNG GLOBAL CORP [KR]; EUNSUNG GLOBAL CORP.
inventors: LEE KI SE [KR]; LEE, Ki Se
abstract: Proposed is a handpiece for skin treatment, which includes a main body housing, a needle cartridge, a needle driving part; a needle insertion depth adjustment part, and a fastening member, wherein the needle insertion depth adjustment part includes a first driving motor seated in the main body housing, a gear disposed on a shaft of the first driving motor, and a fastening nut seated in the main body housing, engaged with the gear, and configured to move rectilinearly forward toward the needle cartridge and backward therefrom according to an operation of the first driving motor.

title: ULTRASONIC TRANSDUCERS AND WIDE-AREA TRANSCRANIAL SONICATION DEVICE INCLUDING THE SAME
assignee: CHOO SEHEUN [KR]; Choo Seheun
inventors: CHOO SEHEUN [KR]; Choo, Seheun
abstract: Disclosed are a dry-coupling ultrasonic transducer that can deliver either focused and non-focused ultrasound to the brain without the use of hydrogel or degassed water as coupling media, while minimizing acoustic distortion, and a wide-area transcranial ultrasound sonication device that incorporates the ultrasonic transducer. The ultrasonic transducer includes a main body configured to generate ultrasound and a dry acoustic coupler configured to transmit the ultrasound to a sonication target, wherein the coupler includes a plurality of flexible coupling pins extending from the front surface of the transducer main body, to provide direct contact with the sonication target.

title: SYSTEM AND METHOD FOR CONTROLLING BEVERAGE BREWING
assignee: VERSUNI HOLDING BV [NL]; VERSUNI HOLDING B.V.
inventors: DE VRIES TIM [NL]; SINNEMA ANKE GERDA [NL]; DE VRIES, Tim et al.
abstract: Provided is a system (50), method and related computer program, for controlling a fluid displacement assembly (18) of a beverage brewing apparatus (10). A wetting volume of liquid (14), to be delivered to a brewing vessel (16) containing raw material (12), is obtained at least partly based on an amount of the raw material from which a beverage is to be prepared. The fluid displacement assembly is operated in a first mode to deliver the wetting volume of the liquid to the brewing vessel containing the raw material. The fluid displacement assembly is switched from operating in the first mode to operating in a second mode, subject, e.g. in response, to the wetting volume of the liquid having been delivered to the brewing vessel by operation of the fluid displacement assembly in the first mode. Fluid is displaced by the fluid displacement assembly being operated in the second mode but at a lower flow rate compared to when the fluid displacement assembly is operated in the first mode. Further provided is the beverage brewing apparatus including the system.

title: FLOW-CONTROLLED HYBRID MACHINE FOR PREPARING HOT COFFEE BEVERAGES
assignee: KOEPPLINGER FLORIAN [DE]; SEYFERT MAXIMILIAN [DE]; KÖPPLINGER, Florian et al.
inventors: KÖPPLINGER FLORIAN [DE]; SEYFERT MAXIMILIAN [DE]; KÖPPLINGER, Florian et al.
abstract: The invention described is equally suitable for preparing pour-over coffee and espresso coffee. In contrast to conventional machines, the flow rate through the coffee powder can be varied and adjusted by means of brewing profiles. In addition to the through-flow, the time and the target weight for terminating the brewing profile can also be set. The target weight is determined by means of a preferably integrated weighing device. The parameters of the brewing process are stored in brewing profiles. These brewing profiles can be stored on the machine. They are created via a smartphone app and are sent to the machine. The app and the machine communicate via Bluetooth.

title: A COFFEE MAKER
assignee: ARCELIK AS [TR]; ARCELIK ANONIM SIRKETI
inventors: BEDIR ERSIN [TR]; KOKEL BILGEHAN [TR]; KURTOGLU YUCEL [TR] et al.
abstract: The present invention relates to a coffee maker (1) comprising a brewing pot (2) wherein the coffee-water mixture is present; a channel (17) which is provided at the base (8) of the brewing pot (2), which has one end opening into the brewing pot (2) and the other end opening outward and which ensures the transfer of the coffee-water mixture in the brewing pot (2) to the external environment; a valve (5) which opens and closes the channel (17); a sealing member (7) which is fixed to the valve (5), which is positioned between the valve (5) and the channel (17), which closes the channel (17) in a sealed manner when the valve (5) is closed and which allows the coffee-water mixture to leave the brewing pot (2) when open; an actuator (3) which moves the valve (5) and the sealing member (7) thereon in the vertical plane to open and close the channel (17); a base (8) which is provided under the channel (17), whereon the valve (5) is fixed, which has at least two openings (12) and which is inclined downward toward the openings (12); and a reservoir (4) having a front wall (9), a rear wall (10), and side walls (11) connecting the front (9) and rear walls (10), all surrounding the base (8) peripherally.

title: METHOD FOR PREPARING A BEVERAGE FROM A CAPSULE, USE OF A BEVERAGE PREPARATION MACHINE AND A CAPSULE, AND SYSTEM FOR PREPARING A BEVERAGE

A brewing chamber disposed below is fluidly connected to the upper reservoir. The brewing chamber has a lower sidewall that extends from the middle opening to a discharge opening disposed at the bottom. The upper reservoir facilitates the controlled pour of hot water over ground coffee disposed in the brewing chamber. The brewing chamber ensures enhanced flavor extraction from the brewed coffee with little to no bypass. The design of the upper reservoir and brewing chamber enhances and expedites the brewing process, making the coffee maker ideal for coffee enthusiasts seeking a high-quality pour-over coffee experience.

abstract: [0000]    Embodiments described herein relate to apparatuses and methods for brewing tea via pods. The pods can be configured to work with existing pod-based espresso machines to build up pressure within the pod thereby enabling better brewing of teas (e.g., matcha, chai, etc.). The pods can have interlocking ribs or tabs configured to interface with the espresso machine to prevent leakage during the brewing process. The pods can be completely biodegradable (e.g., compostable). In some embodiments, the pods can include bilayer films/meshes disposed at the top and bottom of the body of the pod. In some embodiments, the bilayer films include non-woven PLA fabrics and papers with specific mesh sizes configured to allow the existing espresso machines to pierce the films and enable brewing of the teas. The tea powders (e.g., matcha powder) disposed within the pods have specific meshes to enable proper dispersion in water without agglomeration of the matcha particles in the finished beverage.

abstract: A modular countertop beverage brewing system and method configured to rapidly extract cold brew beverages from a brewable mixture. Three independent brewing modes are integrated, two of which regulate temperature. The modes are room temperature, chilling, and heating. Brewing is achieved through a three factored approach: the combination of pressure, water jet nozzle, and a beverage recirculation loop followed by an optional chilling system, and optional heating after brewing. The system enables forced dispense, temperature control options, and programmable brew strength. Unlike conventional systems, this device achieves cold brew in minutes rather than hours, consolidating brewing, strength, and temperature functions in a single compact unit.

The temperature sampling assembly comprises a mounting frame and a temperature measurement member. The mounting frame is connected to the fixing member, the temperature measurement member is disposed on the mounting frame, and the temperature measurement member is configured to measure the temperature of the battery cell. The temperature sampling assembly further comprises an abutting member, the abutting member is connected to the mounting frame, the abutting member is located on the side of the mounting frame facing the battery cell and abuts against the battery cell, and the abutting member has a greater compression rebound rate than the mounting frame. assignee: LANANTE LEONARDO ALISASIS [US]; KIM JEONGKI [US]; DINAN ESMAEL HEJAZI [US] et al.

abstract: A station (STA) receives, from an access point (AP) and via a primary channel (PCH), a first trigger frame indicating a first random access resource unit (RA-RU). The STA decrements a first orthogonal frequency division multiple access (OFDMA) backoff (OBO) counter for the PCH. The STA determines that a physical protocol data unit (PPDU) being received via the PCH comprises an inter-basic service set (BSS) PPDU. Based on the determining, the STA switches from the PCH to a non-primary channel access (NPCA) PCH. The STA initializes a second OBO counter for the NPCA PCH. In response to a second trigger frame, indicating a second RA-RU and received via the NPCA PCH, the STA maintains a value of the first OBO counter and decrements the second OBO counter.

abstract: A disaster relief device is provided that integrates power generation, water purification, and communications into a single portable housing. The device includes a water treatment system having reusable pre-filters, multi-stage sediment filters, a reverse osmosis unit, and a UV sterilizer, together with a clean water storage tank mounted on a drawer for field servicing. An electrical subsystem incorporates a lithium iron phosphate battery pack in a fire-resistant enclosure, a charge controller, and a pure sine wave inverter. A hybrid solar array, including hinged and slide-out panels, provides rapid renewable power deployment, with guided cable carriers ensuring reliability. The housing is sealed to an IP66 rating, reinforced to function as a Faraday cage, and configured for transport with folding handles and airless wheels. Optional features include an integrated rainwater harvesting system, modular interconnection of multiple units for scaling capacity, and a satellite communications bracket for emergency connectivity.
