This method will help and serve almost all consumers from birth to near-death around the world save time and money, optimize the way they spend their money, helps poor people and reduces the poverty rate, eliminates waste in various industries from production to consumption, therefore, is environmentally friendly and low-carbon and effectively respond to global climate change.

title: CIRCUIT BOARD HAVING A SWITCH AND METHOD FOR FABRICATING THE SAME
assignee: HONGQISHENG PREC ELECTRONICS QINHUANGDAO CO LTD [CN]; AVARY HOLDING SHENZHEN CO LTD [CN]; GARUDA TECH CO LTD [TW] et al.
inventors: LI CHENG-JIA [CN]; LI, Cheng-Jia
abstract: [0000]    A circuit board having a switch includes an insulation material layer, first and second insulation layers, a circular channel, a first cover layer, an inert liquid material, a first conductive block, a second conductive block and a conductive layer. The first and second insulation layers are respectively on bottom and top surfaces of the insulation material layer. The circular channel is in the first and second insulation layers and the insulation material layer, and includes a first sub-channel, a second sub-channel, a first connection channel and a second connection channel. The first cover layer is on a bottom surface of the first insulation layer. The inert liquid material is in the circular channel. The first conductive block is in the first sub-channel. The second conductive block is in the second sub-channel. The conductive layer is in the insulation material layer and adjacent to the circular channel.

title: METHODS TO IMPROVE DNA PRODUCTION
assignee: GE PREC HEALTHCARE LLC [US]; GE Precision Healthcare LLC
inventors: NELSON JOHN RICHARD [US]; KVAM ERIK LEEMING [US]; LOWERY LISA ANNE [US] et al.
abstract: [0000]    A method for producing deoxyribonucleic acid (DNA) includes providing a circular DNA template. The method also includes conducting in vitro an amplification reaction on the circular DNA template to generate a DNA product that is rolling circle amplified, wherein a reaction fold-amplification is minimized to enhance protein expression from the DNA product, and wherein a rolling circle amplification reaction is conducted with at least one of (i) the rolling circle amplification reaction being conducted at 15 degrees Celsius to less than 25 degrees Celsius to minimize the reaction fold-amplification, (ii) the rolling circle amplification reaction being conducted with an input concentration of the circular DNA template being greater than 1 nanogram per milliliter to minimize the reaction-fold amplification, or (iii) the rolling circle amplification reaction being conducted with input concentrations of deoxyribonucleoside triphosphates being titrated to 1.2 millimolar or less per deoxynucleotide to minimize the reaction-fold amplification.

title: SWING ARM COMPONENT FOR TOY CAR
assignee: LIN QIAYONG [CN]; LIN QIAYONG
inventors: LIN QIAYONG [CN]; LIN, QIAYONG
abstract: [0000]    A swing arm assembly for a toy car is provided, which includes a housing, a gear train, a light strip, a circular brush disc, and two brush blades. The housing includes a cavity and a communication port that communicates with the cavity. Each gear is rotatably provided on the housing. The light strip is connected to the housing. The circular brush disc includes a first slip ring and a second slip ring, and the first slip ring is located on an outside of the second slip ring. The two brush blades are both provided on the housing, one brush blade is attached to the first slip ring and the other one is attached to the second slip ring. During a rotation of the housing relative to the circular brush disc, the two brush blades are respectively kept in contact with corresponding first slip ring and the second slip ring.

title: A CELLULAR RANGE EXTENDER
assignee: NGOMA TELECOMS TECH [CD]; VISAGIE PIETER JOHAN [ZA]; NGOMA TELECOMS TECHNOLOGIES et al.
inventors: TSHINYAM KAPEND FRANCK [CD]; TSHINYAM KAPEND, Franck
abstract: A cellular range extender has a primary antenna configured to communicate with a cellular network transmitter, a secondary antenna configured to repeat a signal received from the primary antenna, and control circuitry to interconnect the primary and secondary antennas. The primary antenna includes a circular reflector having a first diameter, a circular radiator having a second diameter smaller than the first diameter, and four circular directors, wherein a first director has a third diameter smaller than the second diameter and second to fourth directors have a fourth diameter smaller than the third diameter, the second to fourth directors being axially spaced apart. The reflector, radiator, and the directors are all coaxial. The reflector and the directors are electrically connected or connectable to each other and the radiator is electrically isolated from the reflector and the directors. The cellular range extender has a sensitivity of the primary antenna of as low as -155 dBm.

title: DENTAL IMPLANT
assignee: NOVEL LAB AG [CH]; NOVEL LAB AG
inventors: SHAKARCHI KOBI [IL]; KRASET SHAI [IL]; SHAKARCHI, Kobi et al.
abstract: The present disclosure relates to an implant fixture (100) for implanting in an alveolus of a jawbone. The implant fixture comprises a coronal section (101) defining a proximal end (110), an apical section (104) defining a distal end (120), a head section (102) adjacent the coronal section (101) and a shaft section (103) adjacent the apical section (104), the coronal section, the head section, the shaft section and the apical section forming a single continuous fixture. The head section (102) is defined by a first cross section (200) perpendicular to a longitudinal axis of the implant fixture (100), a centre of the first cross section (200) coinciding with the longitudinal axis of the implant fixture (100). The first cross section (200) is formed of: a substantially circular section (210) having a circumference centred on the centre of the first cross section, and a first and second periphery sections (220, 230) extending around a portion of the circumference of the circular section (210) on opposite sides, each of the first and second periphery sections (220, 230) being defined by an outer contour having a convex portion (221, 231) substantially parallel to the circumference of the circular section (210), and a first and second side portions (222, 223, 232, 233) each extending between each end of the convex portion (221, 231) and the circumference of the circular section (210).

title: GENE EDITING TOOLS AND COMPOSITIONS FOR CELL ENGINEERING
assignee: INTEGRA THERAPEUTICS [ES]; INTEGRA THERAPEUTICS
inventors: PETAZZI PAOLO [ES]; SÁNCHEZ-MEJÍAS GARCIA AVENCIA [ES]; PETAZZI, Paolo et al.
abstract: The present invention relates to a composition comprising a transposase, optionally a RNA-guided nuclease, and a supercoiled circular dsDNA molecule comprising a transgene flanked by ITRs, in particular for its use as a gene editing tool.

title: ROUND PIPE TO FLAT VENT ADAPTER FOR VAPOR MITIGATION IMPLEMENTATIONS
assignee: VAPOR REMEDIATION TECH [US]; Vapor Remediation Technology
inventors: HATTON THOMAS E [US]; BROOKES JR DOUGLAS A [US]; KOCH MATT [US] et al.
abstract: [0000]    Couplers and adapters for vapor mitigation implementation and systems for mitigating vapor are described herein. An example adapter includes a body defining a first section and a second section. The first section defines a first aperture that fluidically couples the first section with a first vapor mitigation conduit. The second section is in fluid communication with the first section and defines a second aperture that fluidically couples the second section with a second vapor mitigation conduit. At least a portion of the first section defines a circular cross-sectional shape, and at least a portion of the second section defines a rectangular cross-sectional shape. At least a portion of the second section defines a trapezoidal shape that evenly transitions a substantially rectangular airstream to substantially circular airstream.

title: LED FILAMENT LAMP
assignee: SIGNIFY HOLDING BV [NL]; SIGNIFY HOLDING B.V.
inventors: DE BEST ANNA [NL]; VAN BOMMEL TIES [NL]; DE BEST, Anna et al.
abstract: A LED filament lamp comprises a plurality of M first LED filaments arranged parallel with each other along a circular first path having a first diameter, D1, and a plurality of O second LED filaments arranged parallel with each other along a circular second path having a second diameter, D2, inside the first path. Each first LED filament is spaced apart from neighboring first LED filaments by a first gap having a first spacing distance, S1. Each second LED filament is spaced apart from neighboring second LED filaments by a second gap having a second spacing distance, S2. A light-transmissive envelope at least partly encloses the plurality of LED filaments, and D2<D1 and S2<S1 and M≥O.

title: Chimeric Antigen Receptors Targeting CD19
assignee: ORNA THERAPEUTICS INC [US]; RENAGADE THERAPEUTICS MAN INC [US]; Orna Therapeutics, Inc. et al.
inventors: DALKILIC-LIDDLE ISIN [US]; MABRY ROBERT [US]; LEE THOMAS [US] et al.
abstract: Provided herein are RNA sequences encoding a chimeric antigen receptor (CAR) comprising a CD19 antigen binding molecule, along with related compositions and methods. In some embodiments, circular RNAs encoding the CD19 binding molecule or CD19 CAR, along with related compositions, methods, and precursors, are described herein. In some embodiments, these compositions comprise a transfer vehicle, e.g., comprising an ionizable lipid described herein.

title: Complex microbial inoculant containing brachybacterium and application of complex microbial inoculant
assignee: CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP; SINOPEC SHANGHAI ENG CO LTD; UNIV SICHUAN et al.
inventors: HONG XIA; LIU YIMING; YAO JIAN et al.
abstract: The invention relates to the field of microbial engineering, and discloses a complex microbial inoculant containing brachybacterium and application of the complex microbial inoculant. The invention relates to a composite bacterial agent, which contains paracoccus (Paracoccus), Prostecobacter (Prostecobacter) and Ideonella (Ideonella). The complex microbial inoculant has high utilization rate of organic acid (especially valeric acid), and can rapidly adapt to mixed organic acid dominated by valeric acid for PHA synthesis, so that the raw material source of PHA is widened, and large-scale production of PHA is facilitated.

title: TRANSFORMED STRAIN AND METHOD FOR DEGRADING PET BY USING THE SAME
assignee: UNIV NAT CHENG KUNG [TW]; National Cheng Kung University
inventors: NG I-SON [TW]; YU JIE-YAO [TW]; TING WAN-WEN [TW] et al.
abstract: The present invention provides a transformed strain, comprising: a host cell; a first nucleotide sequence, located inside the host cell, the first nucleotide sequence encoding a PETase, which is derived from Ideonella; and a second nucleotide sequence, located inside the host cell, the second nucleotide sequence comprising a first chaperon nucleotide sequence or a second chaperon nucleotide sequence; wherein the first chaperon nucleotide sequence encodes a molecular chaperon protein (GroELS), and the second chaperon nucleotide sequence encodes a lipase secretion chaperone protein (LsC). The present invention also provides a method for degrading plastic, using said transformed strain for degrading a plastic having PET.

title: Use of Mesorhizobium oceanicum A novel plastics-degrading enzyme and Use thereof
assignee: KOREA RES INST BIOSCIENCE & BIOTECHNOLOGY [KR]; 한국생명공학연구원
inventors: DAE HEE LEE; SEUNG GOO LEE; KIL KOANG KWON et al.
abstract: A strain of Mesorhizobium oceanicum that decomposes plastic is provided. The strain of Mesorhizobium oceanicum of the present invention has the activity of decomposing plastic and has an excellent ability to grow using plastic as a carbon source, and therefore has the advantage of effectively improving and replacing strains including strains of Ideonella sakaienssis that decomposes PET and Flavobacterium sp. that decomposes Nylon6, which have been reported in the past.

title: Novel Pseudarthrobacter sp. and the use thereof
assignee: KOREA RES INST BIOSCIENCE & BIOTECHNOLOGY [KR]; 한국생명공학연구원
inventors: DAE HEE LEE; SEUNG GOO LEE; KIL KOANG KWON et al.
abstract: Provided is a Pseudarthrobacter sp. N13 strain deposited under accession number KCTC 15177BP. The novel pseudoasrobacter sp. strain of the present invention has an activity of degrading PET and has excellent growing ability by using PET as a carbon source, compared to an ideonella sakaiensis strain, which is known to have an activity of degrading PET in the related art, thereby effectively improving and replacing the conventional ideonella sakaiensis strain.

title: Strain for degrading PET (polyethylene terephthalate) and application of strain in one-step synthesis of vanillin
assignee: UNIV JIANGNAN; 江南大学
inventors: YAN ZHENGFEI; WU JING; ZHAO XIAOMIN et al.
abstract: The invention discloses a bacterial strain for degrading PET (polyethylene terephthalate) and application of the bacterial strain in one-step synthesis of vanillin, and belongs to the technical field of microbial engineering. According to the present invention, in Ideonella sakaiensis LETBE 2022, the mutant strain capable of synthesizing vanillin is obtained through the exogenous expression of a synthesis module composed of a catechol oxygen site methyltransferase (COMT) derived from Rattus norveensis, a carboxylic acid reductase (CAR) derived from Nocardia iowensis, and a phosphopentenyl transferase (SFP) derived from Bacillus subtilis, wherein the synthesis module is composed of the catechol oxygen site methyltransferase (COMT) derived from Rattus norveensis, the carboxylic acid reductase (CAR) derived from Nocardia iowensis, and the phosphopentenyl transferase (SFP) derived from Bacillus subtilis; the conversion from PET to vanillin is realized, the upgrading and recycling from PET to vanillin are realized, and the method has better application potential.

title: IDEONELLA SAKAIENSIS PET-HYDROLASE VARIANT WITH INCREASED THERMOSTABILITY
assignee: CYCLEZYME AB [SE]; CycleZyme AB
inventors: FALCK PETER [SE]; LINARES-PATÉN JAVIER ANTONINO [SE]; Falck, Peter et al.
abstract: [0000]    The present disclosure relates to engineered polypeptides capable of degrading a polymer, such as polyethylene terephthalate (PET). The present disclosure also discloses polynucleotides encoding the polypeptides, vectors comprising the polynucleotides, as well as cells expressing the polynucleotides or vectors comprising the polynucleotides. Disclosed are also methods of degrading polymers, such as PET, and methods of manufacturing terephthalic acid and ethylene glycol.
